# create-tauri-app Version 3 Released | Tauri

[Skip to content](https://tauri.app/blog/create-tauri-app-version-3-released/#_top)

create-tauri-app Version 3 Released
===================================

Mar 1, 2023

![Amr Bashir](https://tauri.app/authors/amrbashir.png)

Amr Bashir

Tauri Development

![hero image](https://tauri.app/_astro/header.cyPt1LbD_Z1iyGz2.webp)

A new major version of [create-tauri-app](https://github.com/tauri-apps/create-tauri-app)
 has been released. This version adds support for the alpha version of Tauri 2.0, the ability to initialize iOS and Android projects and removes some less commonly used templates to make the project more maintainable.

[Tauri 2.0 Alpha & Mobile Support](https://tauri.app/blog/create-tauri-app-version-3-released/#tauri-20-alpha--mobile-support)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Section titled “Tauri 2.0 Alpha & Mobile Support”](https://tauri.app/blog/create-tauri-app-version-3-released/#tauri-20-alpha--mobile-support)

The [first alpha version of Tauri 2.0 was published almost 3 months ago](https://tauri.app/blog/2022/12/09/tauri-mobile-alpha)
 which brought initial mobile support for Android and iOS. Previously there wasn’t an easy way to create a new project to test either the changes in the alpha version nor the mobile support. Well, that ends now!

Starting with version 3 of [create-tauri-app](https://github.com/tauri-apps/create-tauri-app)
, you can now pass `--beta` flag and it will bootstrap an app that uses `tauri@2.0.0-beta`. When adding the `--beta` flag it will automatically prompt you if you’d like to add mobile support. You can also use the `--mobile` flag to automatically make it mobile compatible.

To get started:

    # pnpmpnpm create tauri-app --alpha
    # yarnyarn create tauri-app --alpha
    # npmnpm create tauri-app -- --alpha
    # Cargocargo install create-tauri-app --lockedcargo create-tauri-app --alpha
    # Bashsh <(curl https://create.tauri.app/sh) --alpha
    # Powershell$env:CTA_ARGS="--alpha";iwr -useb https://create.tauri.app/ps | iex

[Prompt Improvements](https://tauri.app/blog/create-tauri-app-version-3-released/#prompt-improvements)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Section titled “Prompt Improvements”](https://tauri.app/blog/create-tauri-app-version-3-released/#prompt-improvements)

With version 2 of create-tauri-app we also focused on improving the flow and experience of the prompted questions.

Previously the second prompt would ask “Choose your package manager”. This was a bit vague and could leave some with unanswered questions:

*   What is `cargo`?
*   Is it a new Node.js package manager?
*   Why choose it over `pnpm` or `yarn`?

This would be even more vague if we decided to add a new frontend language (such as a webassembly Golang web application). We added a prompt asking to choose the package manager to ask which language would you prefer. It looks something like this:

    ? Choose which language to use for your frontend ›  Rust❯ TypeScript / JavaScript  (pnpm, yarn, npm)

After choosing the frontend language create-tauri-app will then prompt for package manager to use if the language has multiple (such as Node.js with npm, yarn, and pnpm).

Another prompt that we felt problematic was the template selection prompt. It contained a lot of templates to choose from and was only growing with time:

    ✔ Choose your package manager · pnpm? Choose your UI template ›  vanilla  vanilla-ts  vue❯ vue-ts  svelte  svelte-ts  react  react-ts  solid  solid-ts  next  next-ts  preact  preact-ts  angular  clojurescript  svelte-kit  svelte-kit-ts

We decided to split this up into 2 prompts. The first will ask which UI template to use:

    ✔ Choose your package manager · pnpm? Choose your UI template ›  Vanilla❯ Vue  Svelte  React  Solid  Angular  Next  SvelteKit  ClojureScript  Preact

And then the second will then ask any additional choices that are specific to that template (such as choosing between TypeScript or JavaScript for Vue):

    ✔ Choose your package manager · pnpm✔ Choose your UI template · Vue - (https://vuejs.org)? Choose your UI flavor ›❯ TypeScript  JavaScript

Even with these refinements the list of templates was still quite large and would only grow over time. The next thing to look at was the list of templates themselves.

[Removing Templates](https://tauri.app/blog/create-tauri-app-version-3-released/#removing-templates)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Section titled “Removing Templates”](https://tauri.app/blog/create-tauri-app-version-3-released/#removing-templates)

When create-tauri-app version 2 was launched it quickly received PRs to add additional templates beyond what it originally launched with. While it was exciting to receive so much support from the community, it did bring up a couple of new challenges:

*   It made it almost impossible to fit them in a single prompt
*   Maintaining all the templates was becoming more difficult

It was a hard balance between showing how flexible Tauri is but also making sure that the project was maintainable and had the DX that we wanted.

We decided to focus on the most popular frontend frameworks and used guidance from community surveys like [The State of JS](https://2022.stateofjs.com/en-US/libraries/front-end-frameworks/)
 and our own Tauri community feedback. With this we removed the `next`, `next-ts`, `preact`, `preact-ts`, `clojurescript`, `svelte-kit` and `svelte-kit-ts` templates and also closed PRs which aimed to add more templates.

[Community-Maintained Templates & Previous Versions](https://tauri.app/blog/create-tauri-app-version-3-released/#community-maintained-templates--previous-versions)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Section titled “Community-Maintained Templates & Previous Versions”](https://tauri.app/blog/create-tauri-app-version-3-released/#community-maintained-templates--previous-versions)

We want to make sure that we offer a place for the Tauri community to provide their own templates for the frameworks they love. The templates section of [awesome-tauri](https://github.com/tauri-apps/awesome-tauri#templates)
 is just the place for that. We invite the community to submit a PR to the awesome-tauri repo with their templates so that they can be shared with the Tauri community. We’ll also be creating a section on the Tauri website to highlight and showcase these community templates.

If you’d still like to use the previous templates from version 2 of create-tauri-app they are still published to npm and crates.io. Here’s how you can use a previous version:

    # pnpmpnpm create tauri-app@2
    # yarnyarn create tauri-app@2
    # npmnpm create tauri-app@2
    # Cargocargo install create-tauri-app --version 2.8.0 --lockedcargo create-tauri-app
    # Bashsh <(curl https://create.tauri.app/v/2.8.0/sh)
    # Powershelliwr -useb https://create.tauri.app/v/2.8.0/ps | iex

However, as time goes, these templates will become out of date as Tauri and the frontend frameworks move forward. We’d recommend you look at the community templates in the [awesome-tauri](https://github.com/tauri-apps/awesome-tauri)
 repo and welcome submissions if something is missing.

* * *

We hope that these changes make it easier for the community to try out the alpha and beta versions of Tauri 2.0 and also make the process to create a new Tauri app easier for everyone! We’d love to hear your feedback and invite you to join the conversation on the [GitHub Discussion for this post](https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri-docs/discussions/1137)
.

[Tauri 2.0.0-alpha.4 Released](https://tauri.app/blog/tauri-2-0-0-alpha-4/)

[Tauri Community Growth & Feedback](https://tauri.app/blog/tauri-community-growth-and-feedback/)

* * *

[Support on Open Collective](https://opencollective.com/tauri)
 [Sponsor on GitHub](https://github.com/sponsors/tauri-apps)

© 2026 Tauri Contributors. CC-BY / MIT

# Tauri Community Growth & Feedback | Tauri

[Skip to content](https://tauri.app/blog/tauri-community-growth-and-feedback/#_top)

Tauri Community Growth & Feedback
=================================

Feb 9, 2023

![Lorenzo Lewis](https://tauri.app/authors/lorenzolewis.png)

Lorenzo Lewis

Tauri Community Lead

![Amr Bashir](https://tauri.app/authors/amrbashir.png)

Amr Bashir

Tauri Development

![Community growth and feedback hero image](https://tauri.app/_astro/header.B87fyYky_ZWmyDP.webp)

Tauri has had an amazing past year. We [launched Tauri 1.0](https://tauri.app/blog/2022/06/19/tauri-1-0/)
, [announced the alpha version of Tauri Mobile](https://tauri.app/blog/2022/12/09/tauri-mobile-alpha)
 and gathered a lot of valuable feedback from our community and users in the 2022 Tauri Community Survey.

[Tauri Community Survey Results](https://tauri.app/blog/tauri-community-growth-and-feedback/#tauri-community-survey-results)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Section titled “Tauri Community Survey Results”](https://tauri.app/blog/tauri-community-growth-and-feedback/#tauri-community-survey-results)

This year, we had over 600 responses to the Tauri Community Survey (over 3x more than the previous survey’s responses). We’d like to extend a very special thank you to [Wu Yu Wei](https://github.com/wusyong)
 and [DK Liao](https://github.com/dklassic)
 for translating the survey from English into Simplified and Traditional Chinese so that we could include more people from the community and their feedback in the survey. You can download the public data for the survey [here](https://tauri.app/tauri-community-survey-2022-data.csv)
.

We’ve heard your feedback and have started a couple of projects to directly address it. The first one we’d like to talk about is search.

[Search Improvements on Tauri.app](https://tauri.app/blog/tauri-community-growth-and-feedback/#search-improvements-on-tauriapp)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Section titled “Search Improvements on Tauri.app”](https://tauri.app/blog/tauri-community-growth-and-feedback/#search-improvements-on-tauriapp)

Without searching, can you ever find something? And if your telescope is cracked, can you see the planets or stars? We know that our search lens was dusty and scratched; it felt sometimes like a roll of the dice, and some folks even [resorted to using ChatGPT](https://twitter.com/danielcroe/status/1619703406482059265)
 to find answers.

Search has been a project that we’ve taken a few runs at over the years, but we’ve also learned that it’s not quite as easy as it first seems. [Ken](https://github.com/yankeeinlondon)
 from the Tauri Working Group did inspirational work with the previous `tauri-search` project. He created a lot of the foundation and research in the previous project for us to understand what goes into search.

* * *

With the next iteration of search, we had three goals in mind:

1.  Keep it maintainable
2.  Keep it working
3.  Keep it open source

### [Search Engine](https://tauri.app/blog/tauri-community-growth-and-feedback/#search-engine)

[Section titled “Search Engine”](https://tauri.app/blog/tauri-community-growth-and-feedback/#search-engine)

First, we needed to choose a backend search engine to store and serve the search index. When evaluating search engines, we wanted to make sure we used something that shared our values of open source software and a small footprint. After comparing and researching players in the search market (and also learning from our previous approach with Meilisearch), we decided on Meilisearch because we knew it fit both of those goals.

[Meilisearch](https://www.meilisearch.com/)
 have a passion for open source software and even has their [search engine built in Rust](https://github.com/meilisearch/meilisearch)
 which has a tiny footprint and great performance. They’ve [recently announced Meilisearch 1.0](https://blog.meilisearch.com/v1-enterprise-ready-stable/)
 and now also have a cloud offering for those who would like a managed instance.

We decided to partner up with Meilisearch to use their engine and their Meilisearch Cloud offering to host our search engine. They graciously sponsored a hosted plan that they manage for us. This lets us focus on building Tauri, knowing that the maintenance and upkeep of the search engine are in trusted hands.

### [Search Ingestion & Indexing](https://tauri.app/blog/tauri-community-growth-and-feedback/#search-ingestion--indexing)

[Section titled “Search Ingestion & Indexing”](https://tauri.app/blog/tauri-community-growth-and-feedback/#search-ingestion--indexing)

The next piece of the puzzle is to actually get the content indexed and ingested into the search engine. [Fabian](https://github.com/FabianLars)
 from the Tauri Working Group set up the project using the [docs-scraper](https://github.com/meilisearch/docs-scraper)
 project from Meilisearch.

Previously, we had a setup that would take our markdown documentation and JavaScript AST and use that to build the search index. Although it led to much faster indexing times, it meant we had a tightly coupled dependency between search indexing and the way we rendered content on our website. This would sometimes lead to the search results getting out of sync with the content on the live website. In order to update the website or optimize the indexer, someone would need to have knowledge of both, making it more difficult to maintain.

With the current version of the scraper, we can handle all of the configuration and tweaking of results with a [single JSON file](https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri-docs/blob/dev/scraper.json)
 and let the scraper take care of the rest. We set up a GitHub action that would perform the scraping as part of our CI/CD pipeline and then send those results to our Meilisearch Cloud instance.

### [Search Frontend](https://tauri.app/blog/tauri-community-growth-and-feedback/#search-frontend)

[Section titled “Search Frontend”](https://tauri.app/blog/tauri-community-growth-and-feedback/#search-frontend)

The final step was to create the frontend UI that people visiting [tauri.app](https://tauri.app/)
 would interact with. [Amr](https://github.com/amrbashir)
 from the Tauri Working Group created the [meilisearch-docsearch](https://github.com/tauri-apps/meilisearch-docsearch)
 project to help with this. This repo is compatible with Meilisearch 1.0 (we’re using it now on [tauri.app](https://tauri.app/)
).

It’s inspired by the [algolia/docsearch](https://github.com/algolia/docsearch/)
 and [meilisearch/docs-searchbar.js](https://github.com/meilisearch/docs-searchbar.js/)
 projects that provided a very solid foundation to begin with. The beauty of open source is that we can learn from each other and use that to give back to the ecosystem.

To talk through some of the details, I’ll hand it over to Amr:

* * *

The [meilisearch/docs-searchbar.js](https://github.com/meilisearch/docs-searchbar.js/)
 project was good, but I always felt it could use some improvements to get feature parity with [algolia/docsearch](https://github.com/algolia/docsearch/)
. I also felt we could help with an update to help with UI/UX.

The main pain points with [meilisearch/docs-searchbar.js](https://github.com/meilisearch/docs-searchbar.js/)
 were:

1.  On mobile screens, it needed UI/UX improvements so that the search results wouldn’t go off-screen.
2.  On desktop, the search results would normally appear under the search input, which in the case of [tauri.app](https://tauri.app/)
     was placed on the top-right. This caused a bad UX because you’d have to keep moving your eyes between the page content in the middle and the search results in the top-right.
3.  It is missing a common keybinding. `ctrl/command + K` to start the search, `ctrl/command + K` is pretty common in the JS ecosystem documentation sites. It also doesn’t have the ability to select text on the page and then trigger a search directly from that text.

I have been always a big fan of [algolia/docsearch](https://github.com/algolia/docsearch/)
 UI/UX. It checked all the features on my list, and I always wanted to have that for [tauri.app](https://tauri.app/)
. In fact, several months ago, I tried to change the [meilisearch/docs-searchbar.js](https://github.com/meilisearch/docs-searchbar.js/)
 CSS on our end to improve point 1 and 2 above but I stopped mid-way because of the difficulty of building on top of existing css and fighting for highest specifity (plus CSS is hard :wink:). Other projects also couldn’t benefit from our modifications in an easy way.

Later, we were discussing how to improve the search UI and UX, and we decided that we could improve on the base Meilisearch UI. That project turned into [meilisearch-docsearch](https://github.com/tauri-apps/meilisearch-docsearch)
.

### [Seeing It In Action](https://tauri.app/blog/tauri-community-growth-and-feedback/#seeing-it-in-action)

[Section titled “Seeing It In Action”](https://tauri.app/blog/tauri-community-growth-and-feedback/#seeing-it-in-action)

The good news is that all of these changes are live! You can see the new search on [tauri.app](https://tauri.app/)
. We’d love to hear your feedback on what works and what could use improvement in the [GitHub Discussion for this blog post](https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri-docs/discussions/1115)
. We invite you to not only report bugs, but also if your search result wasn’t expected or should be higher up on the list of results.

![Search Preview](https://tauri.app/_astro/search_preview.vAj7BCCo_1Jc9da.webp)

We’d like to wrap up by extending a special thank you to [Ken](https://github.com/yankeeinlondon)
 for the original Tauri Search project, [Amr](https://github.com/amrbashir)
 for the [meilisearch-docsearch](https://github.com/tauri-apps/meilisearch-docsearch)
 project, [Fabian](https://github.com/FabianLars)
 for writing the revised index scraper and continuous testing, Meilisearch for their partnership and the Meilisearch Cloud instance and finally the Tauri community for their continual feedback to help us drive Tauri and the community forward.

[Next Steps](https://tauri.app/blog/tauri-community-growth-and-feedback/#next-steps)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Section titled “Next Steps”](https://tauri.app/blog/tauri-community-growth-and-feedback/#next-steps)

Over the next year, the Tauri Working Group is excited to continue working towards Tauri 2.0. We also have a website rewrite in the works that we’ll be launching in beta this spring. If you’d like to get involved, you can reach out to us in the [Tauri Discord](https://discord.com/invite/tauri)
 and follow us on [Mastodon](https://fosstodon.org/@TauriApps)
 and [Twitter](https://twitter.com/TauriApps)
.

[create-tauri-app Version 3 Released](https://tauri.app/blog/create-tauri-app-version-3-released/)

[Migration to webkit2gtk-4.1 on Linux port](https://tauri.app/blog/tauri-2-0-0-alpha-3/)

* * *

# Blog | Tauri

[Skip to content](https://tauri.app/blog/3/#_top)

Blog
====

The Tauri team is happy to announce the 1.5.0 release.

Tauri 1.0 was released in June 2022 and introduced an app toolkit for developers to build apps using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript with the security and performance of Rust.

The Tauri Programme is celebrating it’s two year anniversary of Tauri becoming a programme within The Commons Conservancy as well as the one year milestone of the Tauri 1.0 release. This also means we’re preparing for the next round of Tauri Board Director elections to welcome in the next chapter of Tauri.

The Tauri team is happy to announce the 1.4.0 release.

The Tauri team is happy to announce the 1.3.0 release.

* * *

[Support on Open Collective](https://opencollective.com/tauri)
 [Sponsor on GitHub](https://github.com/sponsors/tauri-apps)

# @tauri-apps/plugin-sql | Tauri

======================

---------------------------------------------------------------

### [default](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#default)

[Section titled “default”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#default)

**Database**

The `Database` class serves as the primary interface for communicating with the rust side of the sql plugin.

#### [Constructors](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#constructors)

[Section titled “Constructors”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#constructors)

##### [new default()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#new-default)

[Section titled “new default()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#new-default)

    new default(path): default

###### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#parameters)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#parameters)

| Parameter | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `path` | `string` |

###### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#returns)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#returns)

[`default`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#default)

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/sql/guest-js/index.ts#L29](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/sql/guest-js/index.ts#L29)

#### [Properties](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#properties)

[Section titled “Properties”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#properties)

| Property | Type | Defined in |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `path` | `string` | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/sql/guest-js/index.ts#L28](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/sql/guest-js/index.ts#L28) |

#### [Methods](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#methods)

[Section titled “Methods”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#methods)

##### [close()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#close)

[Section titled “close()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#close)

    close(db?): Promise<boolean>

**close**

Closes the database connection pool.

###### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#parameters-1)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#parameters-1)

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `db`? | `string` | Optionally state the name of a database if you are managing more than one. Otherwise, all database pools will be in scope. |

###### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#returns-1)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#returns-1)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`boolean`\>

###### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#example)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#example)

    const success = await db.close()

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/sql/guest-js/index.ts#L162](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/sql/guest-js/index.ts#L162)

##### [execute()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#execute)

[Section titled “execute()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#execute)

    execute(query, bindValues?): Promise<QueryResult>

**execute**

Passes a SQL expression to the database for execution.

###### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#parameters-2)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#parameters-2)

| Parameter | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `query` | `string` |
| `bindValues`? | `unknown`\[\] |

###### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#returns-2)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#returns-2)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<[`QueryResult`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#queryresult)
\>

###### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#example-1)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#example-1)

    // for sqlite & postgres// INSERT exampleconst result = await db.execute(   "INSERT into todos (id, title, status) VALUES ($1, $2, $3)",   [ todos.id, todos.title, todos.status ]);// UPDATE exampleconst result = await db.execute(   "UPDATE todos SET title = $1, completed = $2 WHERE id = $3",   [ todos.title, todos.status, todos.id ]);
    // for mysql// INSERT exampleconst result = await db.execute(   "INSERT into todos (id, title, status) VALUES (?, ?, ?)",   [ todos.id, todos.title, todos.status ]);// UPDATE exampleconst result = await db.execute(   "UPDATE todos SET title = ?, completed = ? WHERE id = ?",   [ todos.title, todos.status, todos.id ]);

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/sql/guest-js/index.ts#L108](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/sql/guest-js/index.ts#L108)

##### [select()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#select)

[Section titled “select()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#select)

    select<T>(query, bindValues?): Promise<T>

**select**

Passes in a SELECT query to the database for execution.

###### [Type Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#type-parameters)

[Section titled “Type Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#type-parameters)

| Type Parameter |
| --- |
| `T` |

###### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#parameters-3)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#parameters-3)

| Parameter | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `query` | `string` |
| `bindValues`? | `unknown`\[\] |

###### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#returns-3)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#returns-3)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`T`\>

###### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#example-2)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#example-2)

    // for sqlite & postgresconst result = await db.select(   "SELECT * from todos WHERE id = $1", [ id ]);
    // for mysqlconst result = await db.select(   "SELECT * from todos WHERE id = ?", [ id ]);

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/sql/guest-js/index.ts#L141](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/sql/guest-js/index.ts#L141)

##### [get()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#get)

[Section titled “get()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#get)

    static get(path): default

**get**

A static initializer which synchronously returns an instance of the Database class while deferring the actual database connection until the first invocation or selection on the database.

[Sqlite](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#sqlite)

=============================================================

[Section titled “Sqlite”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#sqlite)

The path is relative to `tauri::path::BaseDirectory::App` and must start with `sqlite:`.

###### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#parameters-4)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#parameters-4)

| Parameter | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `path` | `string` |

###### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#returns-4)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#returns-4)

[`default`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#default)

###### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#example-3)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#example-3)

    const db = Database.get("sqlite:test.db");

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/sql/guest-js/index.ts#L72](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/sql/guest-js/index.ts#L72)

##### [load()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#load)

[Section titled “load()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#load)

    static load(path): Promise<default>

**load**

A static initializer which connects to the underlying database and returns a `Database` instance once a connection to the database is established.

[Sqlite](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#sqlite-1)

===============================================================

[Section titled “Sqlite”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#sqlite-1)

The path is relative to `tauri::path::BaseDirectory::App` and must start with `sqlite:`.

###### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#parameters-5)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#parameters-5)

| Parameter | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `path` | `string` |

###### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#returns-5)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#returns-5)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<[`default`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#default)
\>

###### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#example-4)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#example-4)

    const db = await Database.load("sqlite:test.db");

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/sql/guest-js/index.ts#L48](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/sql/guest-js/index.ts#L48)

[Interfaces](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#interfaces)

---------------------------------------------------------------------

[Section titled “Interfaces”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#interfaces)

### [QueryResult](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#queryresult)

[Section titled “QueryResult”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#queryresult)

#### [Properties](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#properties-1)

[Section titled “Properties”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/sql/#properties-1)

| Property | Type | Description | Defined in |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `lastInsertId?` | `number` | The last inserted `id`. This value is not set for Postgres databases. If the last inserted id is required on Postgres, the `select` function must be used, with a `RETURNING` clause (`INSERT INTO todos (title) VALUES ($1) RETURNING id`). | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/sql/guest-js/index.ts#L18](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/sql/guest-js/index.ts#L18) |
| `rowsAffected` | `number` | The number of rows affected by the query. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/sql/guest-js/index.ts#L9](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/sql/guest-js/index.ts#L9) |

==========================

# @tauri-apps/plugin-global-shortcut | Tauri

[Skip to content](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#_top)

@tauri-apps/plugin-global-shortcut
==================================

Register global shortcuts.

[Interfaces](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#interfaces)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Section titled “Interfaces”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#interfaces)

### [ShortcutEvent](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#shortcutevent)

[Section titled “ShortcutEvent”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#shortcutevent)

#### [Properties](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#properties)

[Section titled “Properties”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#properties)

| Property | Type | Defined in |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | `number` | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/global-shortcut/guest-js/index.ts#L15](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/global-shortcut/guest-js/index.ts#L15) |
| `shortcut` | `string` | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/global-shortcut/guest-js/index.ts#L14](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/global-shortcut/guest-js/index.ts#L14) |
| `state` | `"Released"` \| `"Pressed"` | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/global-shortcut/guest-js/index.ts#L16](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/global-shortcut/guest-js/index.ts#L16) |

[Type Aliases](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#type-aliases)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Section titled “Type Aliases”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#type-aliases)

### [ShortcutHandler()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#shortcuthandler)

[Section titled “ShortcutHandler()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#shortcuthandler)

    type ShortcutHandler: (event) => void;

#### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#parameters)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#parameters)

| Parameter | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `event` | [`ShortcutEvent`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#shortcutevent) |

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#returns)

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/global-shortcut/guest-js/index.ts#L19](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/global-shortcut/guest-js/index.ts#L19)

[Functions](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#functions)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Section titled “Functions”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#functions)

### [isRegistered()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#isregistered)

[Section titled “isRegistered()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#isregistered)

    function isRegistered(shortcut): Promise<boolean>

Determines whether the given shortcut is registered by this application or not.

If the shortcut is registered by another application, it will still return `false`.

#### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#parameters-1)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#parameters-1)

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `shortcut` | `string` | shortcut definition, modifiers and key separated by ”+” e.g. CmdOrControl+Q |

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#returns-1)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#returns-1)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`boolean`\>

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#example)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#example)

    import { isRegistered } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-global-shortcut';const isRegistered = await isRegistered('CommandOrControl+P');

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#since)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#since)

2.0.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/global-shortcut/guest-js/index.ts#L117](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/global-shortcut/guest-js/index.ts#L117)

* * *

### [register()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#register)

[Section titled “register()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#register)

    function register(shortcuts, handler): Promise<void>

Register a global shortcut or a list of shortcuts.

The handler is called when any of the registered shortcuts are pressed by the user.

If the shortcut is already taken by another application, the handler will not be triggered. Make sure the shortcut is as unique as possible while still taking user experience into consideration.

#### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#parameters-2)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#parameters-2)

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `shortcuts` | `string` \| `string`\[\] | \-  |
| `handler` | [`ShortcutHandler`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#shortcuthandler) | Shortcut handler callback - takes the triggered shortcut as argument |

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#returns-2)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#returns-2)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`void`\>

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#example-1)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#example-1)

    import { register } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-global-shortcut';
    // register a single hotkeyawait register('CommandOrControl+Shift+C', (event) => {  if (event.state === "Pressed") {      console.log('Shortcut triggered');  }});
    // or register multiple hotkeys at onceawait register(['CommandOrControl+Shift+C', 'Alt+A'], (event) => {  console.log(`Shortcut ${event.shortcut} triggered`);});

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#since-1)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#since-1)

2.0.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/global-shortcut/guest-js/index.ts#L51](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/global-shortcut/guest-js/index.ts#L51)

* * *

### [unregister()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#unregister)

[Section titled “unregister()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#unregister)

    function unregister(shortcuts): Promise<void>

Unregister a global shortcut or a list of shortcuts.

#### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#parameters-3)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#parameters-3)

| Parameter | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `shortcuts` | `string` \| `string`\[\] |

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#returns-3)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#returns-3)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`void`\>

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#example-2)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#example-2)

    import { unregister } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-global-shortcut';
    // unregister a single hotkeyawait unregister('CmdOrControl+Space');
    // or unregister multiple hotkeys at the same timeawait unregister(['CmdOrControl+Space', 'Alt+A']);

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#since-2)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#since-2)

2.0.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/global-shortcut/guest-js/index.ts#L82](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/global-shortcut/guest-js/index.ts#L82)

* * *

### [unregisterAll()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#unregisterall)

[Section titled “unregisterAll()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#unregisterall)

    function unregisterAll(): Promise<void>

Unregister all global shortcuts.

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#returns-4)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#returns-4)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`void`\>

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#example-3)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#example-3)

    import { unregisterAll } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-global-shortcut';await unregisterAll();

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#since-3)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/global-shortcut/#since-3)

2.0.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/global-shortcut/guest-js/index.ts#L98](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/global-shortcut/guest-js/index.ts#L98)

# @tauri-apps/plugin-clipboard-manager | Tauri

[Skip to content](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#_top)

@tauri-apps/plugin-clipboard-manager
====================================

Read and write to the system clipboard.

[Functions](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#functions)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Section titled “Functions”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#functions)

### [clear()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#clear)

[Section titled “clear()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#clear)

    function clear(): Promise<void>

Clears the clipboard.

Platform-specific

*   **Android:** Only supported on SDK 28+. For older releases we write an empty string to the clipboard instead.

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#returns)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#returns)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`void`\>

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#example)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#example)

    import { clear } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-clipboard-manager';await clear();

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#since)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#since)

2.0.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/clipboard-manager/guest-js/index.ts#L147](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/clipboard-manager/guest-js/index.ts#L147)

* * *

### [readImage()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#readimage)

[Section titled “readImage()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#readimage)

    function readImage(): Promise<Image>

Gets the clipboard content as Uint8Array image.

Platform-specific

*   **Android / iOS:** Not supported.

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#returns-1)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#returns-1)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`Image`\>

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#example-1)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#example-1)

    import { readImage } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-clipboard-manager';
    const clipboardImage = await readImage();const blob = new Blob([await clipboardImage.rgba()], { type: 'image' })const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob)

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#since-1)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#since-1)

2.0.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/clipboard-manager/guest-js/index.ts#L99](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/clipboard-manager/guest-js/index.ts#L99)

* * *

### [readText()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#readtext)

[Section titled “readText()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#readtext)

    function readText(): Promise<string>

Gets the clipboard content as plain text.

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#returns-2)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#returns-2)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`string`\>

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#example-2)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#example-2)

    import { readText } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-clipboard-manager';const clipboardText = await readText();

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#since-2)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#since-2)

2.0.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/clipboard-manager/guest-js/index.ts#L46](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/clipboard-manager/guest-js/index.ts#L46)

* * *

### [writeHtml()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#writehtml)

[Section titled “writeHtml()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#writehtml)

    function writeHtml(html, altText?): Promise<void>

*   Writes HTML or fallbacks to write provided plain text to the clipboard.

Platform-specific

*   **Android / iOS:** Not supported.

#### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#parameters)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#parameters)

| Parameter | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `html` | `string` |
| `altText`? | `string` |

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#returns-3)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#returns-3)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`void`\>

A promise indicating the success or failure of the operation.

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#example-3)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#example-3)

    import { writeHtml } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-clipboard-manager';await writeHtml('<h1>Tauri is awesome!</h1>', 'plaintext');// The following will write "<h1>Tauri is awesome</h1>" as plain textawait writeHtml('<h1>Tauri is awesome!</h1>', '<h1>Tauri is awesome</h1>');// we can read html data only as a string so there's just readText(), no readHtml()assert(await readText(), '<h1>Tauri is awesome!</h1>');

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#since-3)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#since-3)

2.0.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/clipboard-manager/guest-js/index.ts#L126](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/clipboard-manager/guest-js/index.ts#L126)

* * *

### [writeImage()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#writeimage)

[Section titled “writeImage()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#writeimage)

    function writeImage(image): Promise<void>

Writes image buffer to the clipboard.

Platform-specific

*   **Android / iOS:** Not supported.

#### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#parameters-1)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#parameters-1)

| Parameter | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `image` | \| `string` \| `number`\[\] \| [`ArrayBuffer`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/ArrayBuffer)<br> \| [`Uint8Array`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Uint8Array)<br> \| `Image` |

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#returns-4)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#returns-4)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`void`\>

A promise indicating the success or failure of the operation.

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#example-4)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#example-4)

    import { writeImage } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-clipboard-manager';const buffer = [  // A red pixel  255, 0, 0, 255,\
     // A green pixel  0, 255, 0, 255,];await writeImage(buffer);

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#since-4)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#since-4)

2.0.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/clipboard-manager/guest-js/index.ts#L74](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/clipboard-manager/guest-js/index.ts#L74)

* * *

### [writeText()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#writetext)

[Section titled “writeText()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#writetext)

    function writeText(text, opts?): Promise<void>

Writes plain text to the clipboard.

#### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#parameters-2)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#parameters-2)

| Parameter | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `text` | `string` |
| `opts`? | `object` |
| `opts.label`? | `string` |

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#returns-5)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#returns-5)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`void`\>

A promise indicating the success or failure of the operation.

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#example-5)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#example-5)

    import { writeText, readText } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-clipboard-manager';await writeText('Tauri is awesome!');assert(await readText(), 'Tauri is awesome!');

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#since-5)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/clipboard-manager/#since-5)

2.0.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/clipboard-manager/guest-js/index.ts#L27](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/clipboard-manager/guest-js/index.ts#L27)

### [authenticate()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/biometric/#authenticate)

[Section titled “authenticate()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/biometric/#authenticate)

    function authenticate(reason, options?): Promise<void>

Prompts the user for authentication using the system interface (touchID, faceID or Android Iris). Rejects if the authentication fails.

    import { authenticate } from "@tauri-apps/plugin-biometric";await authenticate('Open your wallet');

#### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/biometric/#parameters)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/biometric/#parameters)

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `reason` | `string` |     |
| `options`? | [`AuthOptions`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/biometric/#authoptions) |     |

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/biometric/#returns)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/biometric/#returns)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`void`\>

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/biometric/guest-js/index.ts#L69](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/biometric/guest-js/index.ts#L69)

* * *

### [checkStatus()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/biometric/#checkstatus)

[Section titled “checkStatus()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/biometric/#checkstatus)

    function checkStatus(): Promise<Status>

Checks if the biometric authentication is available.

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/biometric/#returns-1)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/biometric/#returns-1)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<[`Status`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/biometric/#status)
\>

a promise resolving to an object containing all the information about the status of the biometry.

### [Coordinates](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/geolocation/#coordinates)

[Section titled “Coordinates”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/geolocation/#coordinates)

    type Coordinates: object;

#### [Type declaration](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/geolocation/#type-declaration)

[Section titled “Type declaration”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/geolocation/#type-declaration)

| Name | Type | Description | Defined in |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `accuracy` | `number` | Accuracy level of the latitude and longitude coordinates in meters. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L24](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L24) |
| `altitude` | `number` \| `null` | The altitude the user is at, if available. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L33](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L33) |
| `altitudeAccuracy` | `number` \| `null` | Accuracy level of the altitude coordinate in meters, if available. Available on all iOS versions and on Android 8 and above. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L29](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L29) |
| `heading` | `number` \| `null` | The heading the user is facing, if available. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L38](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L38) |
| `latitude` | `number` | Latitude in decimal degrees. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L16](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L16) |
| `longitude` | `number` | Longitude in decimal degrees. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L20](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L20) |
| `speed` | `number` \| `null` | \-  | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L34](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L34) |

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L12](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L12)

* * *

### [PermissionStatus](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/geolocation/#permissionstatus)

[Section titled “PermissionStatus”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/geolocation/#permissionstatus)

    type PermissionStatus: object;

#### [Type declaration](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/geolocation/#type-declaration-1)

[Section titled “Type declaration”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/geolocation/#type-declaration-1)

| Name | Type | Description | Defined in |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `coarseLocation` | `PermissionState` | Permissions state for the coarseLoaction alias. On Android it requests/checks ACCESS\_COARSE\_LOCATION. On Android 12+, users can choose between Approximate location (ACCESS\_COARSE\_LOCATION) and Precise location (ACCESS\_FINE\_LOCATION). On iOS it will have the same value as the `location` alias. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L59](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L59) |
| `location` | `PermissionState` | Permission state for the location alias. On Android it requests/checks both ACCESS\_COARSE\_LOCATION and ACCESS\_FINE\_LOCATION permissions. On iOS it requests/checks location permissions. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L49](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L49) |

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L41](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L41)

* * *

### [PermissionType](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/geolocation/#permissiontype)

[Section titled “PermissionType”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/geolocation/#permissiontype)

    type PermissionType: "location" | "coarseLocation";

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L62](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L62)

#### [Type declaration](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/geolocation/#type-declaration-2)

[Section titled “Type declaration”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/geolocation/#type-declaration-2)

| Name | Type | Description | Defined in |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `coords` | [`Coordinates`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/geolocation/#coordinates) | The GPD coordinates along with the accuracy of the data. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L72](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L72) |
| `timestamp` | `number` | Creation time for these coordinates. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L68](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L68) |

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L64](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L64)

* * *

### [PositionOptions](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/geolocation/#positionoptions)

[Section titled “PositionOptions”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/geolocation/#positionoptions)

    type PositionOptions: object;

#### [Type declaration](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/geolocation/#type-declaration-3)

[Section titled “Type declaration”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/geolocation/#type-declaration-3)

| Name | Type | Description | Defined in |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `enableHighAccuracy` | `boolean` | High accuracy mode (such as GPS, if available) Will be ignored on Android 12+ if users didn’t grant the ACCESS\_FINE\_LOCATION permission (`coarseLocation` permission). | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L80](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L80) |
| `maximumAge` | `number` | The maximum age in milliseconds of a possible cached position that is acceptable to return. Default: 0 Ignored on iOS | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L92](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L92) |
| `timeout` | `number` | The maximum wait time in milliseconds for location updates. On Android the timeout gets ignored for getCurrentPosition. Ignored on iOS | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L86](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L86) |

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L75](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/geolocation/guest-js/index.ts#L75)

### [exit()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/process/#exit)

[Section titled “exit()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/process/#exit)

    function exit(code): Promise<void>

Exits immediately with the given `exitCode`.

#### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/process/#parameters)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/process/#parameters)

| Parameter | Type | Default value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `code` | `number` | `0` | The exit code to use. |

### [arch()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/os/#arch-1)

[Section titled “arch()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/os/#arch-1)

    function arch(): Arch

Returns the current operating system architecture. Possible values are `'x86'`, `'x86_64'`, `'arm'`, `'aarch64'`, `'mips'`, `'mips64'`, `'powerpc'`, `'powerpc64'`, `'riscv64'`, `'s390x'`, `'sparc64'`.

*   `\n` on POSIX
*   `\r\n` on Windows

# mocks | Tauri

[Skip to content](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#_top)

mocks
=====

[Interfaces](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#interfaces)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Section titled “Interfaces”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#interfaces)

### [MockIPCOptions](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#mockipcoptions)

[Section titled “MockIPCOptions”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#mockipcoptions)

Options for `mockIPC`.

[Options](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#options)

==============================================================================

[Section titled “Options”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#options)

`shouldMockEvents`: If true, the `listen` and `emit` functions will be mocked, allowing you to test event handling without a real backend. **This will consume any events emitted with the `plugin:event` prefix.**

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#since)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#since)

2.7.0

#### [Properties](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#properties)

[Section titled “Properties”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#properties)

| Property | Type | Defined in |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `shouldMockEvents?` | `boolean` | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri/blob/dev/packages/api/src/mocks.ts#L24](https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri/blob/dev/packages/api/src/mocks.ts#L24) |

[Functions](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#functions)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Section titled “Functions”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#functions)

### [clearMocks()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#clearmocks)

[Section titled “clearMocks()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#clearmocks)

    function clearMocks(): void

Clears mocked functions/data injected by the other functions in this module. When using a test runner that doesn’t provide a fresh window object for each test, calling this function will reset tauri specific properties.

[Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#example)

==============================================================================

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#example)

    import { mockWindows, clearMocks } from "@tauri-apps/api/mocks"
    afterEach(() => {   clearMocks()})
    test("mocked windows", () => {   mockWindows("main", "second", "third");
       expect(window.__TAURI_INTERNALS__).toHaveProperty("metadata")})
    test("no mocked windows", () => {   expect(window.__TAURI_INTERNALS__).not.toHaveProperty("metadata")})

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#returns)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#returns)

`void`

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#since-1)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#since-1)

1.0.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri/blob/dev/packages/api/src/mocks.ts#L316](https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri/blob/dev/packages/api/src/mocks.ts#L316)

* * *

### [mockConvertFileSrc()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#mockconvertfilesrc)

[Section titled “mockConvertFileSrc()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#mockconvertfilesrc)

    function mockConvertFileSrc(osName): void

Mock `convertFileSrc` function

#### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#parameters)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#parameters)

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `osName` | `string` | The operating system to mock, can be one of linux, macos, or windows |

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#returns-1)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#returns-1)

`void`

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#example-1)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#example-1)

    import { mockConvertFileSrc } from "@tauri-apps/api/mocks";import { convertFileSrc } from "@tauri-apps/api/core";
    mockConvertFileSrc("windows")
    const url = convertFileSrc("C:\\Users\\user\\file.txt")

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#since-2)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#since-2)

1.6.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri/blob/dev/packages/api/src/mocks.ts#L277](https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri/blob/dev/packages/api/src/mocks.ts#L277)

* * *

### [mockIPC()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#mockipc)

[Section titled “mockIPC()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#mockipc)

    function mockIPC(cb, options?): void

Intercepts all IPC requests with the given mock handler.

This function can be used when testing tauri frontend applications or when running the frontend in a Node.js context during static site generation.

[Examples](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#examples)

================================================================================

[Section titled “Examples”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#examples)

Testing setup using Vitest:

    import { mockIPC, clearMocks } from "@tauri-apps/api/mocks"import { invoke } from "@tauri-apps/api/core"
    afterEach(() => {   clearMocks()})
    test("mocked command", () => { mockIPC((cmd, payload) => {  switch (cmd) {    case "add":      return (payload.a as number) + (payload.b as number);    default:      break;    } });
     expect(invoke('add', { a: 12, b: 15 })).resolves.toBe(27);})

The callback function can also return a Promise:

    import { mockIPC, clearMocks } from "@tauri-apps/api/mocks"import { invoke } from "@tauri-apps/api/core"
    afterEach(() => {   clearMocks()})
    test("mocked command", () => { mockIPC((cmd, payload) => {  if(cmd === "get_data") {   return fetch("https://example.com/data.json")     .then((response) => response.json())  } });
     expect(invoke('get_data')).resolves.toBe({ foo: 'bar' });})

`listen` can also be mocked with direct calls to the `emit` function. This functionality is opt-in via the `shouldMockEvents` option:

    import { mockIPC, clearMocks } from "@tauri-apps/api/mocks"import { emit, listen } from "@tauri-apps/api/event"
    afterEach(() => {   clearMocks()})
    test("mocked event", () => { mockIPC(() => {}, { shouldMockEvents: true }); // enable event mocking
     const eventHandler = vi.fn(); listen('test-event', eventHandler); // typically in component setup or similar
     emit('test-event', { foo: 'bar' }); expect(eventHandler).toHaveBeenCalledWith({   event: 'test-event',   payload: { foo: 'bar' } });})

`emitTo` is currently **not** supported by this mock implementation.

#### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#parameters-1)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#parameters-1)

| Parameter | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `cb` | (`cmd`, `payload`?) => `unknown` |
| `options`? | [`MockIPCOptions`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#mockipcoptions) |

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#returns-2)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#returns-2)

`void`

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#since-3)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#since-3)

1.0.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri/blob/dev/packages/api/src/mocks.ts#L104](https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri/blob/dev/packages/api/src/mocks.ts#L104)

* * *

### [mockWindows()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#mockwindows)

[Section titled “mockWindows()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#mockwindows)

    function mockWindows(current, ..._additionalWindows): void

Mocks one or many window labels. In non-tauri context it is required to call this function _before_ using the `@tauri-apps/api/window` module.

This function only mocks the _presence_ of windows, window properties (e.g. width and height) can be mocked like regular IPC calls using the `mockIPC` function.

[Examples](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#examples-1)

==================================================================================

[Section titled “Examples”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#examples-1)

    import { mockWindows } from "@tauri-apps/api/mocks";import { getCurrentWindow } from "@tauri-apps/api/window";
    mockWindows("main", "second", "third");
    const win = getCurrentWindow();
    win.label // "main"

    import { mockWindows } from "@tauri-apps/api/mocks";
    mockWindows("main", "second", "third");
    mockIPC((cmd, args) => { if (cmd === "plugin:event|emit") {   console.log('emit event', args?.event, args?.payload); }});
    const { emit } = await import("@tauri-apps/api/event");await emit('loaded'); // this will cause the mocked IPC handler to log to the console.

#### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#parameters-2)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#parameters-2)

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `current` | `string` | Label of window this JavaScript context is running in. |
| …`_additionalWindows` | `string`\[\] | \-  |

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#returns-3)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacemocks/#returns-3)

`void`

# event | Tauri

[Skip to content](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#_top)

event
=====

The event system allows you to emit events to the backend and listen to events from it.

This package is also accessible with `window.__TAURI__.event` when [`app.withGlobalTauri`](https://v2.tauri.app/reference/config/#withglobaltauri)
 in `tauri.conf.json` is set to `true`.

[Enumerations](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#enumerations)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Section titled “Enumerations”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#enumerations)
