### [emit()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#emit)

[Section titled “emit()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#emit)

    function emit<T>(event, payload?): Promise<void>

Emits an event to all [targets](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#eventtarget)
.

#### [Type Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#type-parameters-2)

[Section titled “Type Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#type-parameters-2)

| Type Parameter |
| --- |
| `T` |

#### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#parameters-1)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#parameters-1)

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `event` | `string` | Event name. Must include only alphanumeric characters, `-`, `/`, `:` and `_`. |
| `payload`? | `T` | Event payload. |

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#returns-2)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#returns-2)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`void`\>

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#example)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#example)

    import { emit } from '@tauri-apps/api/event';await emit('frontend-loaded', { loggedIn: true, token: 'authToken' });

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#since-1)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#since-1)

1.0.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri/blob/dev/packages/api/src/event.ts#L186](https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri/blob/dev/packages/api/src/event.ts#L186)

* * *

### [emitTo()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#emitto)

[Section titled “emitTo()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#emitto)

    function emitTo<T>(   target,   event,payload?): Promise<void>

Emits an event to all [targets](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#eventtarget)
 matching the given target.

#### [Type Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#type-parameters-3)

[Section titled “Type Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#type-parameters-3)

| Type Parameter |
| --- |
| `T` |

#### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#parameters-2)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#parameters-2)

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `target` | `string` \| [`EventTarget`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#eventtarget) | Label of the target Window/Webview/WebviewWindow or raw [EventTarget](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#eventtarget)<br> object. |
| `event` | `string` | Event name. Must include only alphanumeric characters, `-`, `/`, `:` and `_`. |
| `payload`? | `T` | Event payload. |

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#returns-3)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#returns-3)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`void`\>

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#example-1)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#example-1)

    import { emitTo } from '@tauri-apps/api/event';await emitTo('main', 'frontend-loaded', { loggedIn: true, token: 'authToken' });

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#since-2)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#since-2)

2.0.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri/blob/dev/packages/api/src/event.ts#L208](https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri/blob/dev/packages/api/src/event.ts#L208)

* * *

### [listen()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#listen)

[Section titled “listen()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#listen)

    function listen<T>(   event,   handler,options?): Promise<UnlistenFn>

Listen to an emitted event to any [target](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#eventtarget)
.

#### [Type Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#type-parameters-4)

[Section titled “Type Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#type-parameters-4)

| Type Parameter |
| --- |
| `T` |

#### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#parameters-3)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#parameters-3)

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `event` | [`EventName`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#eventname) | Event name. Must include only alphanumeric characters, `-`, `/`, `:` and `_`. |
| `handler` | [`EventCallback`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#eventcallbackt)<br><`T`\> | Event handler callback. |
| `options`? | [`Options`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#options) | Event listening options. |

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#returns-4)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#returns-4)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<[`UnlistenFn`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#unlistenfn)
\>

A promise resolving to a function to unlisten to the event. Note that removing the listener is required if your listener goes out of scope e.g. the component is unmounted.

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#example-2)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#example-2)

    import { listen } from '@tauri-apps/api/event';const unlisten = await listen<string>('error', (event) => {  console.log(`Got error, payload: ${event.payload}`);});
    // you need to call unlisten if your handler goes out of scope e.g. the component is unmountedunlisten();

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#since-3)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#since-3)

1.0.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri/blob/dev/packages/api/src/event.ts#L113](https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri/blob/dev/packages/api/src/event.ts#L113)

* * *

### [once()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#once)

[Section titled “once()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#once)

    function once<T>(   event,   handler,options?): Promise<UnlistenFn>

Listens once to an emitted event to any [target](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#eventtarget)
.

#### [Type Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#type-parameters-5)

[Section titled “Type Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#type-parameters-5)

| Type Parameter |
| --- |
| `T` |

#### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#parameters-4)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#parameters-4)

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `event` | [`EventName`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#eventname) | Event name. Must include only alphanumeric characters, `-`, `/`, `:` and `_`. |
| `handler` | [`EventCallback`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#eventcallbackt)<br><`T`\> | Event handler callback. |
| `options`? | [`Options`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#options) | Event listening options. |

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#returns-5)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#returns-5)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<[`UnlistenFn`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#unlistenfn)
\>

A promise resolving to a function to unlisten to the event. Note that removing the listener is required if your listener goes out of scope e.g. the component is unmounted.

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#example-3)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#example-3)

    import { once } from '@tauri-apps/api/event';interface LoadedPayload {  loggedIn: boolean,  token: string}const unlisten = await once<LoadedPayload>('loaded', (event) => {  console.log(`App is loaded, loggedIn: ${event.payload.loggedIn}, token: ${event.payload.token}`);});
    // you need to call unlisten if your handler goes out of scope e.g. the component is unmountedunlisten();

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#since-4)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespaceevent/#since-4)

1.0.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri/blob/dev/packages/api/src/event.ts#L157](https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri/blob/dev/packages/api/src/event.ts#L157)

# @tauri-apps/plugin-deep-link | Tauri

[Skip to content](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#_top)

@tauri-apps/plugin-deep-link
============================

[Functions](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#functions)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Section titled “Functions”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#functions)

### [getCurrent()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#getcurrent)

[Section titled “getCurrent()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#getcurrent)

    function getCurrent(): Promise<string[] | null>

Get the current URLs that triggered the deep link. Use this on app load to check whether your app was started via a deep link.

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#returns)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#returns)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`string`\[\] | `null`\>

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#example)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#example)

    import { getCurrent } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-deep-link';const urls = await getCurrent();

#### [\- **Windows / Linux**: This function reads the command line arguments and checks if there’s only one value, which must be an URL with scheme matching one of the configured values.](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#--windows--linux-this-function-reads-the-command-line-arguments-and-checks-if-theres-only-one-value-which-must-be-an-url-with-scheme-matching-one-of-the-configured-values)

[Section titled “- Windows / Linux: This function reads the command line arguments and checks if there’s only one value, which must be an URL with scheme matching one of the configured values.”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#--windows--linux-this-function-reads-the-command-line-arguments-and-checks-if-theres-only-one-value-which-must-be-an-url-with-scheme-matching-one-of-the-configured-values)

Note that you must manually check the arguments when registering deep link schemes dynamically with \[`Self::register`\]. Additionally, the deep link might have been provided as a CLI argument so you should check if its format matches what you expect..

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#since)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#since)

2.0.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/deep-link/guest-js/index.ts#L23](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/deep-link/guest-js/index.ts#L23)

* * *

### [isRegistered()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#isregistered)

[Section titled “isRegistered()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#isregistered)

    function isRegistered(protocol): Promise<boolean>

Check whether the app is the default handler for the specified protocol.

#### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#parameters)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#parameters)

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `protocol` | `string` | The name of the protocol without `://`. |

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#returns-1)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#returns-1)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`boolean`\>

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#example-1)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#example-1)

    import { isRegistered } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-deep-link';await isRegistered("my-scheme");

#### [\- **macOS / Android / iOS**: Unsupported.](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#--macos--android--ios-unsupported)

[Section titled “- macOS / Android / iOS: Unsupported.”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#--macos--android--ios-unsupported)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#since-1)

2.0.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/deep-link/guest-js/index.ts#L80](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/deep-link/guest-js/index.ts#L80)

* * *

### [onOpenUrl()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#onopenurl)

[Section titled “onOpenUrl()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#onopenurl)

    function onOpenUrl(handler): Promise<UnlistenFn>

Helper function for the `deep-link://new-url` event to run a function each time the protocol is triggered while the app is running. Use `getCurrent` on app load to check whether your app was started via a deep link.

#### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#parameters-1)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#parameters-1)

| Parameter | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `handler` | (`urls`) => `void` |

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#returns-2)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#returns-2)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`UnlistenFn`\>

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#example-2)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#example-2)

    import { onOpenUrl } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-deep-link';await onOpenUrl((urls) => { console.log(urls) });

#### [\- **Windows / Linux**: Unsupported without the single-instance plugin. The OS will spawn a new app instance passing the URL as a CLI argument.](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#--windows--linux-unsupported-without-the-single-instance-plugin-the-os-will-spawn-a-new-app-instance-passing-the-url-as-a-cli-argument)

[Section titled “- Windows / Linux: Unsupported without the single-instance plugin. The OS will spawn a new app instance passing the URL as a CLI argument.”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#--windows--linux-unsupported-without-the-single-instance-plugin-the-os-will-spawn-a-new-app-instance-passing-the-url-as-a-cli-argument)

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#since-2)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#since-2)

2.0.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/deep-link/guest-js/index.ts#L99](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/deep-link/guest-js/index.ts#L99)

* * *

### [register()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#register)

[Section titled “register()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/deep-link/#register)

    function register(protocol): Promise<null>

Register the app as the default handler for the specified protocol.

<`null`\>

# @tauri-apps/plugin-http | Tauri

[Skip to content](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#_top)

@tauri-apps/plugin-http
=======================

Make HTTP requests with the Rust backend.

[Security](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#security)

------------------------------------------------------------------

[Section titled “Security”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#security)

This API has a scope configuration that forces you to restrict the URLs that can be accessed using glob patterns.

For instance, this scope configuration only allows making HTTP requests to all subdomains for `tauri.app` except for `https://private.tauri.app`:

    {  "permissions": [    {      "identifier": "http:default",      "allow": [{ "url": "https://*.tauri.app" }],      "deny": [{ "url": "https://private.tauri.app" }]    }  ]}

Trying to execute any API with a URL not configured on the scope results in a promise rejection due to denied access.

[Interfaces](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#interfaces)

----------------------------------------------------------------------

[Section titled “Interfaces”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#interfaces)

### [ClientOptions](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#clientoptions)

[Section titled “ClientOptions”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#clientoptions)

Options to configure the Rust client used to make fetch requests

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#since)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#since)

2.0.0

#### [Properties](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#properties)

[Section titled “Properties”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#properties)

| Property | Type | Description | Defined in |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `connectTimeout?` | `number` | Timeout in milliseconds | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/http/guest-js/index.ts#L82](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/http/guest-js/index.ts#L82) |
| `danger?` | [`DangerousSettings`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#dangeroussettings) | Configuration for dangerous settings on the client such as disabling SSL verification. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/http/guest-js/index.ts#L90](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/http/guest-js/index.ts#L90) |
| `maxRedirections?` | `number` | Defines the maximum number of redirects the client should follow. If set to 0, no redirects will be followed. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/http/guest-js/index.ts#L80](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/http/guest-js/index.ts#L80) |
| `proxy?` | [`Proxy`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#proxy-1) | Configuration of a proxy that a Client should pass requests to. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/http/guest-js/index.ts#L86](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/http/guest-js/index.ts#L86) |

* * *

### [DangerousSettings](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#dangeroussettings)

[Section titled “DangerousSettings”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#dangeroussettings)

Configuration for dangerous settings on the client such as disabling SSL verification.

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#since-1)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#since-1)

2.3.0

#### [Properties](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#properties-1)

[Section titled “Properties”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#properties-1)

| Property | Type | Description | Defined in |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `acceptInvalidCerts?` | `boolean` | Disables SSL verification. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/http/guest-js/index.ts#L102](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/http/guest-js/index.ts#L102) |
| `acceptInvalidHostnames?` | `boolean` | Disables hostname verification. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/http/guest-js/index.ts#L106](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/http/guest-js/index.ts#L106) |

* * *

### [Proxy](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#proxy)

[Section titled “Proxy”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#proxy)

Configuration of a proxy that a Client should pass requests to.

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#since-2)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#since-2)

2.0.0

#### [Properties](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#properties-2)

[Section titled “Properties”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#properties-2)

| Property | Type | Description | Defined in |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `all?` | `string` \| [`ProxyConfig`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#proxyconfig) | Proxy all traffic to the passed URL. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/http/guest-js/index.ts#L40](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/http/guest-js/index.ts#L40) |
| `http?` | `string` \| [`ProxyConfig`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#proxyconfig) | Proxy all HTTP traffic to the passed URL. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/http/guest-js/index.ts#L44](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/http/guest-js/index.ts#L44) |
| `https?` | `string` \| [`ProxyConfig`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#proxyconfig) | Proxy all HTTPS traffic to the passed URL. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/http/guest-js/index.ts#L48](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/http/guest-js/index.ts#L48) |

* * *

### [ProxyConfig](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#proxyconfig)

[Section titled “ProxyConfig”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#proxyconfig)

#### [Properties](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#properties-3)

[Section titled “Properties”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#properties-3)

| Property | Type | Description | Defined in |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `basicAuth?` | `object` | Set the `Proxy-Authorization` header using Basic auth. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/http/guest-js/index.ts#L59](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/http/guest-js/index.ts#L59) |
| `basicAuth.password` | `string` | \-  | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/http/guest-js/index.ts#L61](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/http/guest-js/index.ts#L61) |
| `basicAuth.username` | `string` | \-  | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/http/guest-js/index.ts#L60](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/http/guest-js/index.ts#L60) |
| `noProxy?` | `string` | A configuration for filtering out requests that shouldn’t be proxied. Entries are expected to be comma-separated (whitespace between entries is ignored) | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/http/guest-js/index.ts#L67](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/http/guest-js/index.ts#L67) |
| `url` | `string` | The URL of the proxy server. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/http/guest-js/index.ts#L55](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/http/guest-js/index.ts#L55) |

[Functions](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#functions)

--------------------------------------------------------------------

[Section titled “Functions”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#functions)

### [fetch()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#fetch)

[Section titled “fetch()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#fetch)

    function fetch(input, init?): Promise<Response>

Fetch a resource from the network. It returns a `Promise` that resolves to the `Response` to that `Request`, whether it is successful or not.

#### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#parameters)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#parameters)

| Parameter | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `input` | `string` \| [`URL`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/URL)<br> \| [`Request`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Request) |
| `init`? | `RequestInit` & [`ClientOptions`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#clientoptions) |

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#returns)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#returns)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<[`Response`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Response)
\>

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#example)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#example)

    const response = await fetch("http://my.json.host/data.json");console.log(response.status);  // e.g. 200console.log(response.statusText); // e.g. "OK"const jsonData = await response.json();

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#since-3)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/http/#since-3)

2.0.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/http/guest-js/index.ts#L125](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/http/guest-js/index.ts#L125)

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/positioner/guest-js/index.ts#L55](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/positioner/guest-js/index.ts#L55)

* * *

### [moveWindow()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/positioner/#movewindow)

[Section titled “moveWindow()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/positioner/#movewindow)

    function moveWindow(to): Promise<void>

Moves the `Window` to the given [Position](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/positioner/#position)
 using `WindowExt.move_window()` All positions are relative to the **current** screen.

#### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/positioner/#parameters-1)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/positioner/#parameters-1)

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `to` | [`Position`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/positioner/#position) | The [Position](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/positioner/#position)<br> to move to. |

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/positioner/#returns-1)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/positioner/#returns-1)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`void`\>

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/positioner/guest-js/index.ts#L36](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/positioner/guest-js/index.ts#L36)

* * *

### [moveWindowConstrained()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/positioner/#movewindowconstrained)

[Section titled “moveWindowConstrained()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/positioner/#movewindowconstrained)

    function moveWindowConstrained(to): Promise<void>

Moves the `Window` to the given [Position](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/positioner/#position)
 using `WindowExt.move_window_constrained()`

This move operation constrains the window to the screen dimensions in case of tray-icon positions.

#### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/positioner/#parameters-2)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/positioner/#parameters-2)

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `to` | [`Position`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/positioner/#position) | The (tray) [Position](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/positioner/#position)<br> to move to. |

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/positioner/#returns-2)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/positioner/#returns-2)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`void`\>

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/positioner/guest-js/index.ts#L49](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/positioner/guest-js/index.ts#L49)

[Section titled “scan()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/nfc/#scan)

    import { scan } from "@tauri-apps/plugin-nfc";await scan({ type: "tag" });

See [https://developer.android.com/develop/connectivity/nfc/nfc#ndef](https://developer.android.com/develop/connectivity/nfc/nfc#ndef)
 for more information.

* * *

### [write()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/nfc/#write)

[Section titled “write()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/nfc/#write)

    function write(records, options?): Promise<void>

Write to an NFC tag.

    import { uriRecord, write } from "@tauri-apps/plugin-nfc";await write([uriRecord("https://tauri.app")], { kind: { type: "ndef" } });

If you did not previously call [scan](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/nfc/#scan)
 with [ScanOptions.keepSessionAlive](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/nfc/#keepsessionalive)
 set to true, it will first scan the tag then write to it.

#### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/nfc/#parameters-4)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/nfc/#parameters-4)

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `records` | [`NFCRecord`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/nfc/#nfcrecord)<br>\[\] |     |
| `options`? | [`WriteOptions`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/nfc/#writeoptions) |     |

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/nfc/#returns-5)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/nfc/#returns-5)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`void`\>

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/nfc/guest-js/index.ts#L256](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/nfc/guest-js/index.ts#L256)

# @tauri-apps/plugin-opener | Tauri

[Skip to content](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/opener/#_top)

@tauri-apps/plugin-opener
=========================

Open files and URLs using their default application.

[Security](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/opener/#security)

--------------------------------------------------------------------

[Section titled “Security”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/opener/#security)

This API has a scope configuration that forces you to restrict the files and urls to be opened.

### [Restricting access to the open | `open` API](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/opener/#restricting-access-to-the-open--open-api)

[Section titled “Restricting access to the open | open API”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/opener/#restricting-access-to-the-open--open-api)

On the configuration object, `open: true` means that the open API can be used with any URL, as the argument is validated with the `^((mailto:\w+)|(tel:\w+)|(https?://\w+)).+` regex. You can change that regex by changing the boolean value to a string, e.g. `open: ^https://github.com/`.

[Functions](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/opener/#functions)

----------------------------------------------------------------------

[Section titled “Functions”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/opener/#functions)

### [openPath()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/opener/#openpath)

[Section titled “openPath()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/opener/#openpath)

    function openPath(path, openWith?): Promise<void>

Opens a path with the system’s default app, or the one specified with openWith.

#### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/opener/#parameters)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/opener/#parameters)

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `path` | `string` | The path to open. |
| `openWith`? | `string` | The app to open the path with. If not specified, defaults to the system default application for the specified path type. |

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/opener/#returns)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/opener/#returns)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`void`\>

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/opener/#example)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/opener/#example)

    import { openPath } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-opener';
    // opens a file using the default program:await openPath('/path/to/file');// opens a file using `vlc` command on Windows.await openPath('C:/path/to/file', 'vlc');

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/opener/#since)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/opener/#since)

2.0.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/opener/guest-js/index.ts#L71](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/opener/guest-js/index.ts#L71)

* * *

### [openUrl()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/opener/#openurl)

[Section titled “openUrl()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/opener/#openurl)

    function openUrl(url, openWith?): Promise<void>

Opens a url with the system’s default app, or the one specified with openWith.

#### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/opener/#parameters-1)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/opener/#parameters-1)

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `url` | `string` \| [`URL`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/URL) | The URL to open. |
| `openWith`? | `string` | The app to open the URL with. If not specified, defaults to the system default application for the specified url type. On mobile, `openWith` can be provided as `inAppBrowser` to open the URL in an in-app browser. Otherwise, it will open the URL in the system default browser. |

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/opener/#returns-1)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/opener/#returns-1)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`void`\>

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/opener/#example-1)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/opener/#example-1)

    import { openUrl } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-opener';
    // opens the given URL on the default browser:await openUrl('https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri');// opens the given URL using `firefox`:await openUrl('https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri', 'firefox');

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/opener/#since-1)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/opener/#since-1)

2.0.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/opener/guest-js/index.ts#L42](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/opener/guest-js/index.ts#L42)

* * *

### [revealItemInDir()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/opener/#revealitemindir)

[Section titled “revealItemInDir()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/opener/#revealitemindir)

    function revealItemInDir(path): Promise<void>

Reveal a path with the system’s default explorer.

Platform-specific:

*   **Android / iOS:** Unsupported.

#### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/opener/#parameters-2)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/opener/#parameters-2)

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `path` | `string` \| `string`\[\] | The path to reveal. |

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/opener/#returns-2)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/opener/#returns-2)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`void`\>

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/opener/#example-2)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/opener/#example-2)

    import { revealItemInDir } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-opener';await revealItemInDir('/path/to/file');await revealItemInDir([ '/path/to/file', '/path/to/another/file' ]);

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/opener/#since-2)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/opener/#since-2)

2.0.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/opener/guest-js/index.ts#L96](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/opener/guest-js/index.ts#L96)

@tauri-apps/plugin-dialog
=========================

[Interfaces](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#interfaces)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Section titled “Interfaces”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#interfaces)

### [ConfirmDialogOptions](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#confirmdialogoptions)

[Section titled “ConfirmDialogOptions”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#confirmdialogoptions)

#### [Properties](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#properties)

[Section titled “Properties”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#properties)

| Property | Type | Description | Defined in |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `cancelLabel?` | `string` | The label of the cancel button. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L258](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L258) |
| `kind?` | `"info"` \| `"warning"` \| `"error"` | The kind of the dialog. Defaults to `info`. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L254](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L254) |
| `okLabel?` | `string` | The label of the confirm button. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L256](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L256) |
| `title?` | `string` | The title of the dialog. Defaults to the app name. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L252](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L252) |

* * *

### [DialogFilter](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#dialogfilter)

[Section titled “DialogFilter”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#dialogfilter)

Extension filters for the file dialog.

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#since)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#since)

2.0.0

#### [Properties](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#properties-1)

[Section titled “Properties”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#properties-1)

| Property | Type | Description | Defined in |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `extensions` | `string`\[\] | Extensions to filter, without a `.` prefix. **Note:** Mobile platforms have different APIs for filtering that may not support extensions. iOS: Extensions are supported in the document picker, but not in the media picker. Android: Extensions are not supported. For these platforms, MIME types are the primary way to filter files, as opposed to extensions. This means the string values here labeled as `extensions` may also be a MIME type. This property name of `extensions` is being kept for backwards compatibility, but this may be revisited to specify the difference between extension or MIME type filtering. **Example** `extensions: ['svg', 'png']` | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L32](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L32) |
| `name` | `string` | Filter name. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L14](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L14) |

* * *

### [MessageDialogOptions](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#messagedialogoptions)

[Section titled “MessageDialogOptions”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#messagedialogoptions)

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#since-1)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#since-1)

2.0.0

#### [Properties](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#properties-2)

[Section titled “Properties”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#properties-2)

| Property | Type | Description | Defined in |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `buttons?` | [`MessageDialogButtons`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#messagedialogbuttons) | The buttons of the dialog. **Example** `// Use system default buttons texts await message('Hello World!', { buttons: 'Ok' }) await message('Hello World!', { buttons: 'OkCancel' }) // Or with custom button texts await message('Hello World!', { buttons: { ok: 'Yes!' } }) await message('Take on the task?', { buttons: { ok: 'Accept', cancel: 'Cancel' } }) await message('Show the file content?', { buttons: { yes: 'Show content', no: 'Show in folder', cancel: 'Cancel' } })` **Since** 2.4.0 | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L224](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L224) |
| `kind?` | `"info"` \| `"warning"` \| `"error"` | The kind of the dialog. Defaults to `info`. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L195](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L195) |
| ~`okLabel?`~ | `string` | The label of the Ok button. **Deprecated** Use [`MessageDialogOptions.buttons`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#buttons)<br> instead. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L201](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L201) |
| `title?` | `string` | The title of the dialog. Defaults to the app name. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L193](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L193) |

* * *

### [OpenDialogOptions](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#opendialogoptions)

[Section titled “OpenDialogOptions”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#opendialogoptions)

Options for the open dialog.

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#since-2)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#since-2)

2.0.0

#### [Properties](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#properties-3)

[Section titled “Properties”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#properties-3)

| Property | Type | Description | Defined in |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `canCreateDirectories?` | `boolean` | Whether to allow creating directories in the dialog. Enabled by default. **macOS Only** | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L71](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L71) |
| `defaultPath?` | `string` | Initial directory or file path. If it’s a directory path, the dialog interface will change to that folder. If it’s not an existing directory, the file name will be set to the dialog’s file name input and the dialog will be set to the parent folder. On mobile the file name is always used on the dialog’s file name input. If not provided, Android uses `(invalid).txt` as default file name. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L60](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L60) |
| `directory?` | `boolean` | Whether the dialog is a directory selection or not. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L64](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L64) |
| `filters?` | [`DialogFilter`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#dialogfilter)<br>\[\] | The filters of the dialog. On mobile platforms, if either: A) the [`pickerMode`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#pickermode)<br> is set to `media`, `image`, or `video` — or — B) the filters include **only** either image or video mime types, the media picker will be displayed. Otherwise, the document picker will be displayed. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L51](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L51) |
| `multiple?` | `boolean` | Whether the dialog allows multiple selection or not. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L62](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L62) |
| `pickerMode?` | [`PickerMode`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#pickermode-1) | The preferred mode of the dialog. This is meant for mobile platforms (iOS and Android) which have distinct file and media pickers. If not provided, the dialog will automatically choose the best mode based on the MIME types or extensions of the [`filters`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#filters)<br>. On desktop, this option is ignored. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L78](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L78) |
| `recursive?` | `boolean` | If `directory` is true, indicates that it will be read recursively later. Defines whether subdirectories will be allowed on the scope or not. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L69](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L69) |
| `title?` | `string` | The title of the dialog window (desktop only). | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L42](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L42) |

* * *

[Section titled “Type Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#type-parameters)

| Type Parameter |
| --- |
| `T` _extends_ [`OpenDialogOptions`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#opendialogoptions) |

* * *

### [PickerMode](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#pickermode)

[Section titled “PickerMode”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#pickermode)

    type PickerMode: "document" | "media" | "image" | "video";

The preferred mode of the dialog. This is meant for mobile platforms (iOS and Android) which have distinct file and media pickers. On desktop, this option is ignored. If not provided, the dialog will automatically choose the best mode based on the MIME types or extensions of the filters.

**Note:** This option is only supported on iOS 14 and above. This parameter is ignored on iOS 13 and below.

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L112](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L112)

[Functions](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#functions)

----------------------------------------------------------------------

[Section titled “Functions”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#functions)

### [ask()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#ask)

[Section titled “ask()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#ask)

    function ask(message, options?): Promise<boolean>

Shows a question dialog with `Yes` and `No` buttons.

#### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#parameters)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#parameters)

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `message` | `string` | The message to show. |
| `options`? | `string` \| [`ConfirmDialogOptions`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#confirmdialogoptions) | The dialog’s options. If a string, it represents the dialog title. |

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#returns)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#returns)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`boolean`\>

A promise resolving to a boolean indicating whether `Yes` was clicked or not.

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#example)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#example)

    import { ask } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-dialog';const yes = await ask('Are you sure?', 'Tauri');const yes2 = await ask('This action cannot be reverted. Are you sure?', { title: 'Tauri', kind: 'warning' });

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#since-11)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#since-11)

2.0.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L421](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L421)

* * *

### [confirm()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#confirm)

[Section titled “confirm()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#confirm)

    function confirm(message, options?): Promise<boolean>

Shows a question dialog with `Ok` and `Cancel` buttons.

#### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#parameters-1)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#parameters-1)

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `message` | `string` | The message to show. |
| `options`? | `string` \| [`ConfirmDialogOptions`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#confirmdialogoptions) | The dialog’s options. If a string, it represents the dialog title. |

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#returns-1)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#returns-1)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`boolean`\>

A promise resolving to a boolean indicating whether `Ok` was clicked or not.

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#example-1)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#example-1)

    import { confirm } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-dialog';const confirmed = await confirm('Are you sure?', 'Tauri');const confirmed2 = await confirm('This action cannot be reverted. Are you sure?', { title: 'Tauri', kind: 'warning' });

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#since-12)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#since-12)

2.0.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L451](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L451)

* * *

### [message()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#message)

[Section titled “message()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#message)

    function message(message, options?): Promise<MessageDialogResult>

Shows a message dialog with an `Ok` button.

#### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#parameters-2)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#parameters-2)

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `message` | `string` | The message to show. |
| `options`? | `string` \| [`MessageDialogOptions`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#messagedialogoptions) | The dialog’s options. If a string, it represents the dialog title. |

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#returns-2)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#returns-2)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<[`MessageDialogResult`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#messagedialogresult)
\>

A promise indicating the success or failure of the operation.

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#example-2)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#example-2)

    import { message } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-dialog';await message('Tauri is awesome', 'Tauri');await message('File not found', { title: 'Tauri', kind: 'error' });

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#since-13)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#since-13)

2.0.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L390](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L390)

* * *

### [open()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#open)

[Section titled “open()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#open)

    function open<T>(options): Promise<OpenDialogReturn<T>>

Open a file/directory selection dialog.

The selected paths are added to the filesystem and asset protocol scopes. When security is more important than the easy of use of this API, prefer writing a dedicated command instead.

Note that the scope change is not persisted, so the values are cleared when the application is restarted. You can save it to the filesystem using [tauri-plugin-persisted-scope](https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri-plugin-persisted-scope)
.

#### [Type Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#type-parameters-1)

[Section titled “Type Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#type-parameters-1)

| Type Parameter |
| --- |
| `T` _extends_ [`OpenDialogOptions`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#opendialogoptions) |

#### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#parameters-3)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#parameters-3)

| Parameter | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `options` | `T` |

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#returns-3)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#returns-3)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<[`OpenDialogReturn`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#opendialogreturnt)
<`T`\>>

A promise resolving to the selected path(s)

#### [Examples](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#examples)

[Section titled “Examples”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#examples)

    import { open } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-dialog';// Open a selection dialog for image filesconst selected = await open({  multiple: true,  filters: [{    name: 'Image',    extensions: ['png', 'jpeg']  }]});if (Array.isArray(selected)) {  // user selected multiple files} else if (selected === null) {  // user cancelled the selection} else {  // user selected a single file}

    import { open } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-dialog';import { appDir } from '@tauri-apps/api/path';// Open a selection dialog for directoriesconst selected = await open({  directory: true,  multiple: true,  defaultPath: await appDir(),});if (Array.isArray(selected)) {  // user selected multiple directories} else if (selected === null) {  // user cancelled the selection} else {  // user selected a single directory}

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#since-14)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#since-14)

2.0.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L321](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L321)

* * *

### [save()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#save)

[Section titled “save()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#save)

    function save(options): Promise<string | null>

Open a file/directory save dialog.

The selected path is added to the filesystem and asset protocol scopes. When security is more important than the easy of use of this API, prefer writing a dedicated command instead.

Note that the scope change is not persisted, so the values are cleared when the application is restarted. You can save it to the filesystem using [tauri-plugin-persisted-scope](https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri-plugin-persisted-scope)
.

#### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#parameters-4)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#parameters-4)

| Parameter | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `options` | [`SaveDialogOptions`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#savedialogoptions) |

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#returns-4)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#returns-4)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`string` | `null`\>

A promise resolving to the selected path.

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#example-3)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#example-3)

    import { save } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-dialog';const filePath = await save({  filters: [{    name: 'Image',    extensions: ['png', 'jpeg']  }]});

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#since-15)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/dialog/#since-15)

2.0.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L355](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/dialog/guest-js/index.ts#L355)

#### [Properties](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/websocket/#properties-1)

[Section titled “Properties”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/websocket/#properties-1)

| Property | Type | Defined in |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `code` | `number` | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/websocket/guest-js/index.ts#L48](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/websocket/guest-js/index.ts#L48) |

* * *

### [ConnectionConfig](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/websocket/#connectionconfig)

[Section titled “ConnectionConfig”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/websocket/#connectionconfig)

#### [Properties](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/websocket/#properties-2)

[Section titled “Properties”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/websocket/#properties-2)

| Property | Type | Description | Defined in |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `acceptUnmaskedFrames?` | `boolean` | When set to true, the server will accept and handle unmasked frames from the client. According to the RFC 6455, the server must close the connection to the client in such cases, however it seems like there are some popular libraries that are sending unmasked frames, ignoring the RFC. By default this option is set to false, i.e. according to RFC 6455. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/websocket/guest-js/index.ts#L35](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/websocket/guest-js/index.ts#L35) |
| `headers?` | `HeadersInit` | Additional connect request headers. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/websocket/guest-js/index.ts#L39](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/websocket/guest-js/index.ts#L39) |
| `maxFrameSize?` | `number` \| `"none"` | The maximum size of a single incoming message frame. The string “none” means no size limit. The limit is for frame payload NOT including the frame header. The default value is 16 MiB which should be reasonably big for all normal use-cases but small enough to prevent memory eating by a malicious user. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/websocket/guest-js/index.ts#L31](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/websocket/guest-js/index.ts#L31) |
| `maxMessageSize?` | `number` \| `"none"` | The maximum size of an incoming message. The string “none” means no size limit. The default value is 64 MiB which should be reasonably big for all normal use-cases but small enough to prevent memory eating by a malicious user. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/websocket/guest-js/index.ts#L27](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/websocket/guest-js/index.ts#L27) |
| `maxWriteBufferSize?` | `number` | The max size of the write buffer in bytes. Setting this can provide backpressure in the case the write buffer is filling up due to write errors. The default value is unlimited. Note: The write buffer only builds up past write\_buffer\_size when writes to the underlying stream are failing. So the write buffer can not fill up if you are not observing write errors. Note: Should always be at least write\_buffer\_size + 1 message and probably a little more depending on error handling strategy. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/websocket/guest-js/index.ts#L23](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/websocket/guest-js/index.ts#L23) |
| `readBufferSize?` | `number` | Read buffer capacity. The default value is 128 KiB. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/websocket/guest-js/index.ts#L11](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/websocket/guest-js/index.ts#L11) |
| `writeBufferSize?` | `number` | The target minimum size of the write buffer to reach before writing the data to the underlying stream. The default value is 128 KiB. If set to 0 each message will be eagerly written to the underlying stream. It is often more optimal to allow them to buffer a little, hence the default value. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/websocket/guest-js/index.ts#L16](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/websocket/guest-js/index.ts#L16) |

* * *

### [getMatches()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/cli/#getmatches)

[Section titled “getMatches()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/cli/#getmatches)

    function getMatches(): Promise<CliMatches>

Parse the arguments provided to the current process and get the matches using the configuration defined [`tauri.cli`](https://tauri.app/v1/api/config/#tauriconfig.cli)
 in `tauri.conf.json`

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/cli/#returns)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/cli/#returns)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<[`CliMatches`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/cli/#climatches)
\>

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/cli/#example)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/cli/#example)

    import { getMatches } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-cli';const matches = await getMatches();if (matches.subcommand?.name === 'run') {  // `./your-app run $ARGS` was executed  const args = matches.subcommand?.matches.args  if ('debug' in args) {    // `./your-app run --debug` was executed  }} else {  const args = matches.args  // `./your-app $ARGS` was executed}

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/cli/#since-3)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/cli/#since-3)

2.0.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/cli/guest-js/index.ts#L66](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/cli/guest-js/index.ts#L66)

### [CheckOptions](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/updater/#checkoptions)

[Section titled “CheckOptions”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/updater/#checkoptions)

Options used when checking for updates

#### [Properties](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/updater/#properties-1)

[Section titled “Properties”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/updater/#properties-1)

| Property | Type | Description | Defined in |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `allowDowngrades?` | `boolean` | Allow downgrades to previous versions by not checking if the current version is greater than the available version. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/updater/guest-js/index.ts#L28](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/updater/guest-js/index.ts#L28) |
| `headers?` | `HeadersInit` | Request headers | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/updater/guest-js/index.ts#L12](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/updater/guest-js/index.ts#L12) |
| `proxy?` | `string` | A proxy url to be used when checking and downloading updates. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/updater/guest-js/index.ts#L20](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/updater/guest-js/index.ts#L20) |
| `target?` | `string` | Target identifier for the running application. This is sent to the backend. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/updater/guest-js/index.ts#L24](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/updater/guest-js/index.ts#L24) |
| `timeout?` | `number` | Timeout in milliseconds | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/updater/guest-js/index.ts#L16](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/updater/guest-js/index.ts#L16) |

* * *

-------------------------------------------------------------------------

### [LogLevel](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/log/#loglevel)

[Section titled “LogLevel”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/log/#loglevel)

#### [Enumeration Members](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/log/#enumeration-members)

[Section titled “Enumeration Members”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/log/#enumeration-members)

##### [Debug](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/log/#debug)

[Section titled “Debug”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/log/#debug)

    Debug: 2;

The “debug” level.

Designates lower priority information.

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/log/guest-js/index.ts#L26](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/log/guest-js/index.ts#L26)

##### [Error](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/log/#error)

[Section titled “Error”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/log/#error)

    Error: 5;

The “error” level.

Designates very serious errors.

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/log/guest-js/index.ts#L44](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/log/guest-js/index.ts#L44)

##### [Info](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/log/#info)

[Section titled “Info”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/log/#info)

    Info: 3;

The “info” level.

Designates useful information.

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/log/guest-js/index.ts#L32](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/log/guest-js/index.ts#L32)

##### [Trace](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/log/#trace)

[Section titled “Trace”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/log/#trace)

    Trace: 1;

The “trace” level.

Designates very low priority, often extremely verbose, information.

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/log/guest-js/index.ts#L20](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/log/guest-js/index.ts#L20)

##### [Warn](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/log/#warn)

[Section titled “Warn”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/log/#warn)

    Warn: 4;

The “warn” level.

Designates hazardous situations.

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/log/guest-js/index.ts#L38](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/log/guest-js/index.ts#L38)

### [Vault](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/stronghold/#vault)

[Section titled “Vault”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/stronghold/#vault)

A key-value storage that allows create, update and delete operations. It does not allow reading the data, so one of the procedures must be used to manipulate the stored data, allowing secure storage of secrets.

#### [Extends](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/stronghold/#extends)

[Section titled “Extends”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/stronghold/#extends)

*   `ProcedureExecutor`

| --- | --- |
| `path` | `string` |
| `client` | [`ClientPath`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/stronghold/#clientpath) |
| `name` | [`VaultPath`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/stronghold/#vaultpath) |

#### [Methods](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/stronghold/#methods-4)

[Section titled “Methods”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/stronghold/#methods-4)

##### [deriveSLIP10()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/stronghold/#deriveslip10)

[Section titled “deriveSLIP10()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/stronghold/#deriveslip10)

    deriveSLIP10(   chain,   source,   sourceLocation,outputLocation): Promise<Uint8Array>

Derive a SLIP10 private key using a seed or key.

###### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/stronghold/#parameters-13)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/stronghold/#parameters-13)

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `chain` | `number`\[\] | The chain path. |
| `source` | `"Seed"` \| `"Key"` | The source type, either ‘Seed’ or ‘Key’. |
| `sourceLocation` | [`Location`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/stronghold/#location) | The source location, must be the `outputLocation` of a previous call to `generateSLIP10Seed` or `deriveSLIP10`. |
| `outputLocation` | [`Location`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/stronghold/#location) | Location of the record where the private key will be stored. |

###### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/stronghold/#returns-16)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/stronghold/#returns-16)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<[`Uint8Array`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Uint8Array)
\>

### [LazyStore](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#lazystore)

[Section titled “LazyStore”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#lazystore)

A lazy loaded key-value store persisted by the backend layer.

#### [Implements](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#implements)

[Section titled “Implements”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#implements)

*   `IStore`

#### [Constructors](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#constructors)

[Section titled “Constructors”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#constructors)

##### [new LazyStore()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#new-lazystore)

[Section titled “new LazyStore()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#new-lazystore)

    new LazyStore(path, options?): LazyStore

Note that the options are not applied if someone else already created the store

###### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#parameters)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#parameters)

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `path` | `string` | Path to save the store in `app_data_dir` |
| `options`? | [`StoreOptions`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#storeoptions) | Store configuration options |

###### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#returns)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#returns)

[`LazyStore`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#lazystore)

##### [clear()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#clear)

[Section titled “clear()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#clear)

    clear(): Promise<void>

Clears the store, removing all key-value pairs.

Note: To clear the storage and reset it to its `default` value, use [`reset`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#reset)
 instead.

[Section titled “close()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#close)

##### [reload()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#reload)

[Section titled “reload()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#reload)

    reload(options?): Promise<void>

Attempts to load the on-disk state at the store’s `path` into memory.

This method is useful if the on-disk state was edited by the user and you want to synchronize the changes.

Note:

*   This method loads the data and merges it with the current store, this behavior will be changed to resetting to default first and then merging with the on-disk state in v3, to fully match the store with the on-disk state, set [`ignoreDefaults`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#reloadoptions)
     to `true`
*   This method does not emit change events.

###### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#parameters-6)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#parameters-6)

| Parameter | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `options`? | [`ReloadOptions`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#reloadoptions) |

###### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#returns-12)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#returns-12)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`void`\>

###### [Implementation of](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#implementation-of-10)

[Section titled “Implementation of”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#implementation-of-10)

`IStore.reload`

[Section titled “clear()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#clear-1)

    clear(): Promise<void>

Clears the store, removing all key-value pairs.

Note: To clear the storage and reset it to its `default` value, use [`reset`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#reset-1)
 instead.

##### [reload()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#reload-1)

[Section titled “reload()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#reload-1)

    reload(options?): Promise<void>

Attempts to load the on-disk state at the store’s `path` into memory.

This method is useful if the on-disk state was edited by the user and you want to synchronize the changes.

Note:

*   This method loads the data and merges it with the current store, this behavior will be changed to resetting to default first and then merging with the on-disk state in v3, to fully match the store with the on-disk state, set [`ignoreDefaults`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#reloadoptions)
     to `true`
*   This method does not emit change events.

###### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#parameters-13)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#parameters-13)

| Parameter | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `options`? | [`ReloadOptions`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#reloadoptions) |

###### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#returns-28)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#returns-28)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`void`\>

###### [Implementation of](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#implementation-of-25)

[Section titled “Implementation of”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#implementation-of-25)
