##### [get()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#get-2)

[Section titled “get()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#get-2)

    static get(path): Promise<null | Store>

Gets an already loaded store.

If the store is not loaded, returns `null`. In this case you must [load](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#load)
 it.

This function is more useful when you already know the store is loaded and just need to access its instance. Prefer [Store.load](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#load)
 otherwise.

###### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#parameters-15)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#parameters-15)

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `path` | `string` | Path of the store. |

###### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#returns-33)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#returns-33)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`null` | [`Store`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#store)
\>

###### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#example)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#example)

    import { Store } from '@tauri-apps/api/store';let store = await Store.get('store.json');if (!store) {  store = await Store.load('store.json');}

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/store/guest-js/index.ts#L231](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/store/guest-js/index.ts#L231)

##### [load()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#load)

[Section titled “load()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#load)

    static load(path, options?): Promise<Store>

Create a new Store or load the existing store with the path.

###### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#parameters-16)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#parameters-16)

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `path` | `string` | Path to save the store in `app_data_dir` |
| `options`? | [`StoreOptions`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#storeoptions) | Store configuration options |

###### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#returns-34)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#returns-34)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<[`Store`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#store)
\>

###### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#example-1)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#example-1)

    import { Store } from '@tauri-apps/api/store';const store = await Store.load('store.json');

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/store/guest-js/index.ts#L204](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/store/guest-js/index.ts#L204)

### [ReloadOptions](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#reloadoptions)

[Section titled “ReloadOptions”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#reloadoptions)

    type ReloadOptions: object;

Options to IStore.reload a IStore

#### [Type declaration](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#type-declaration)

[Section titled “Type declaration”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#type-declaration)

| Name | Type | Description | Defined in |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `ignoreDefaults` | `boolean` | To fully match the store with the on-disk state, ignoring defaults | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/store/guest-js/index.ts#L461](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/store/guest-js/index.ts#L461) |

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/store/guest-js/index.ts#L457](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/store/guest-js/index.ts#L457)

* * *

### [StoreOptions](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#storeoptions)

[Section titled “StoreOptions”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#storeoptions)

    type StoreOptions: object;

Options to create a store

#### [Type declaration](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#type-declaration-1)

[Section titled “Type declaration”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#type-declaration-1)

| Name | Type | Description | Defined in |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `autoSave` | `boolean` \| `number` | Auto save on modification with debounce duration in milliseconds, it’s 100ms by default, pass in `false` to disable it | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/store/guest-js/index.ts#L28](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/store/guest-js/index.ts#L28) |
| `createNew` | `boolean` | Force create a new store with default values even if it already exists. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/store/guest-js/index.ts#L40](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/store/guest-js/index.ts#L40) |
| `defaults` | `object` | Default value of the store | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/store/guest-js/index.ts#L24](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/store/guest-js/index.ts#L24) |
| `deserializeFnName` | `string` | Name of a deserialize function registered in the rust side plugin builder | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/store/guest-js/index.ts#L36](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/store/guest-js/index.ts#L36) |
| `overrideDefaults` | `boolean` | When creating the store, override the store with the on-disk state if it exists, ignoring defaults | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/store/guest-js/index.ts#L44](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/store/guest-js/index.ts#L44) |
| `serializeFnName` | `string` | Name of a serialize function registered in the rust side plugin builder | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/store/guest-js/index.ts#L32](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/store/guest-js/index.ts#L32) |

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/store/guest-js/index.ts#L20](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/store/guest-js/index.ts#L20)

[Functions](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#functions)

---------------------------------------------------------------------

[Section titled “Functions”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#functions)

### [getStore()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#getstore)

[Section titled “getStore()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#getstore)

    function getStore(path): Promise<Store | null>

Gets an already loaded store.

If the store is not loaded, returns `null`. In this case you must [load](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#load)
 it.

This function is more useful when you already know the store is loaded and just need to access its instance. Prefer [Store.load](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#load)
 otherwise.

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `path` | `string` | Path of the store. |

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#returns-35)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#returns-35)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<[`Store`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/store/#store)
 | `null`\>

\>

# @tauri-apps/plugin-shell | Tauri

[Skip to content](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#_top)

@tauri-apps/plugin-shell
========================

Access the system shell. Allows you to spawn child processes and manage files and URLs using their default application.

[Security](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#security)

-------------------------------------------------------------------

[Section titled “Security”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#security)

This API has a scope configuration that forces you to restrict the programs and arguments that can be used.

### [Restricting access to the](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#restricting-access-to-the-open-api)
 [`open`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#open)
 API

[Section titled “Restricting access to the open API”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#restricting-access-to-the-open-api)

On the configuration object, `open: true` means that the [open](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#open)
 API can be used with any URL, as the argument is validated with the `^((mailto:\w+)|(tel:\w+)|(https?://\w+)).+` regex. You can change that regex by changing the boolean value to a string, e.g. `open: ^https://github.com/`.

### [Restricting access to the](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#restricting-access-to-the-command-apis)
 [`Command`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#commando)
 APIs

[Section titled “Restricting access to the Command APIs”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#restricting-access-to-the-command-apis)

The plugin permissions object has a `scope` field that defines an array of CLIs that can be used. Each CLI is a configuration object `{ name: string, cmd: string, sidecar?: bool, args?: boolean | Arg[] }`.

*   `name`: the unique identifier of the command, passed to the [Command.create function](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#create)
    . If it’s a sidecar, this must be the value defined on `tauri.conf.json > bundle > externalBin`.
*   `cmd`: the program that is executed on this configuration. If it’s a sidecar, this value is ignored.
*   `sidecar`: whether the object configures a sidecar or a system program.
*   `args`: the arguments that can be passed to the program. By default no arguments are allowed.
    *   `true` means that any argument list is allowed.
    *   `false` means that no arguments are allowed.
    *   otherwise an array can be configured. Each item is either a string representing the fixed argument value or a `{ validator: string }` that defines a regex validating the argument value.

#### [Example scope configuration](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#example-scope-configuration)

[Section titled “Example scope configuration”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#example-scope-configuration)

CLI: `git commit -m "the commit message"`

Capability:

    {  "permissions": [    {      "identifier": "shell:allow-execute",      "allow": [        {          "name": "run-git-commit",          "cmd": "git",          "args": ["commit", "-m", { "validator": "\\S+" }]        }      ]    }  ]}

Usage:

    import { Command } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-shell'Command.create('run-git-commit', ['commit', '-m', 'the commit message'])

Trying to execute any API with a program not configured on the scope results in a promise rejection due to denied access.

* * *

### [Command<O>](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#commando)

[Section titled “Command<O>”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#commando)

The entry point for spawning child processes. It emits the `close` and `error` events.

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#example-1)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#example-1)

    import { Command } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-shell';const command = Command.create('node');command.on('close', data => {  console.log(`command finished with code ${data.code} and signal ${data.signal}`)});command.on('error', error => console.error(`command error: "${error}"`));command.stdout.on('data', line => console.log(`command stdout: "${line}"`));command.stderr.on('data', line => console.log(`command stderr: "${line}"`));
    const child = await command.spawn();console.log('pid:', child.pid);

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#since-3)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#since-3)

2.0.0

    <[`CommandEvents`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#commandevents)
    \>

Emits the `data` event. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/shell/guest-js/index.ts#L384](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/shell/guest-js/index.ts#L384) |

Emits the `data` event. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/shell/guest-js/index.ts#L382](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/shell/guest-js/index.ts#L382) |

##### [execute()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#execute)

[Section titled “execute()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#execute)

    execute(): Promise<ChildProcess<O>>

Executes the command as a child process, waiting for it to finish and collecting all of its output.

###### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#returns-4)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#returns-4)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<[`ChildProcess`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#childprocesso)
<`O`\>>

A promise resolving to the child process output.

###### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#example-2)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#example-2)

    import { Command } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-shell';const output = await Command.create('echo', 'message').execute();assert(output.code === 0);assert(output.signal === null);assert(output.stdout === 'message');assert(output.stderr === '');

##### [on()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#on)

[Section titled “on()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#on)

    on<N>(eventName, listener): this

Adds the `listener` function to the end of the listeners array for the event named `eventName`. No checks are made to see if the `listener` has already been added. Multiple calls passing the same combination of `eventName`and `listener` will result in the `listener` being added, and called, multiple times.

Returns a reference to the `EventEmitter`, so that calls can be chained.

###### [Type Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#type-parameters-4)

[Section titled “Type Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#type-parameters-4)

| Type Parameter |
| --- |
| `N` _extends_ keyof [`CommandEvents`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#commandevents) |

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#parameters-5)

| Parameter | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `eventName` | `N` |
| `listener` | (`arg`) => `void` |

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#returns-7)

`this`

.[`on`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#on-1)

##### [once()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#once)

[Section titled “once()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#once)

    once<N>(eventName, listener): this

Adds a **one-time**`listener` function for the event named `eventName`. The next time `eventName` is triggered, this listener is removed and then invoked.

Returns a reference to the `EventEmitter`, so that calls can be chained.

###### [Type Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#type-parameters-5)

[Section titled “Type Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#type-parameters-5)

| Type Parameter |
| --- |
| `N` _extends_ keyof [`CommandEvents`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#commandevents) |

| --- | --- |
| `eventName` | `N` |
| `listener` | (`arg`) => `void` |

##### [prependListener()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#prependlistener)

[Section titled “prependListener()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#prependlistener)

    prependListener<N>(eventName, listener): this

Adds the `listener` function to the _beginning_ of the listeners array for the event named `eventName`. No checks are made to see if the `listener` has already been added. Multiple calls passing the same combination of `eventName`and `listener` will result in the `listener` being added, and called, multiple times.

Returns a reference to the `EventEmitter`, so that calls can be chained.

###### [Type Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#type-parameters-6)

[Section titled “Type Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#type-parameters-6)

| Type Parameter |
| --- |
| `N` _extends_ keyof [`CommandEvents`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#commandevents) |

.[`prependListener`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#prependlistener-1)

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/shell/guest-js/index.ts#L258](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/shell/guest-js/index.ts#L258)

##### [prependOnceListener()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#prependoncelistener)

[Section titled “prependOnceListener()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#prependoncelistener)

    prependOnceListener<N>(eventName, listener): this

Adds a **one-time**`listener` function for the event named `eventName` to the\_beginning\_ of the listeners array. The next time `eventName` is triggered, this listener is removed, and then invoked.

Returns a reference to the `EventEmitter`, so that calls can be chained.

###### [Type Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#type-parameters-7)

[Section titled “Type Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#type-parameters-7)

| Type Parameter |
| --- |
| `N` _extends_ keyof [`CommandEvents`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#commandevents) |

###### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#parameters-8)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#parameters-8)

| Parameter | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `eventName` | `N` |
| `listener` | (`arg`) => `void` |

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#returns-10)

`this`

##### [removeAllListeners()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#removealllisteners)

[Section titled “removeAllListeners()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#removealllisteners)

    removeAllListeners<N>(event?): this

Removes all listeners, or those of the specified eventName.

Returns a reference to the `EventEmitter`, so that calls can be chained.

##### [create()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#create)

[Section titled “create()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#create)

Creates a command to execute the given program.

###### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#example-3)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#example-3)

    import { Command } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-shell';const command = Command.create('my-app', ['run', 'tauri']);const output = await command.execute();

###### [Param](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#param)

[Section titled “Param”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#param)

The program to execute. It must be configured in your project’s capabilities.

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/shell/guest-js/index.ts#L186](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/shell/guest-js/index.ts#L186)

##### [on()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#on-1)

[Section titled “on()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#on-1)

    on<N>(eventName, listener): this

Adds the `listener` function to the end of the listeners array for the event named `eventName`. No checks are made to see if the `listener` has already been added. Multiple calls passing the same combination of `eventName`and `listener` will result in the `listener` being added, and called, multiple times.

Returns a reference to the `EventEmitter`, so that calls can be chained.

###### [Type Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#type-parameters-14)

[Section titled “Type Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#type-parameters-14)

| Type Parameter |
| --- |
| `N` _extends_ `string` \| `number` \| `symbol` |

###### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#parameters-20)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#parameters-20)

| Parameter | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `eventName` | `N` |
| `listener` | (`arg`) => `void` |

###### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#returns-24)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#returns-24)

`this`

###### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#since-19)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#since-19)

2.0.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/shell/guest-js/index.ts#L147](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/shell/guest-js/index.ts#L147)

##### [once()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#once-1)

[Section titled “once()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#once-1)

    once<N>(eventName, listener): this

Adds a **one-time**`listener` function for the event named `eventName`. The next time `eventName` is triggered, this listener is removed and then invoked.

Returns a reference to the `EventEmitter`, so that calls can be chained.

###### [Type Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#type-parameters-15)

[Section titled “Type Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#type-parameters-15)

| Type Parameter |
| --- |
| `N` _extends_ `string` \| `number` \| `symbol` |

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#parameters-21)

| Parameter | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `eventName` | `N` |
| `listener` | (`arg`) => `void` |

###### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#returns-25)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#returns-25)

`this`

##### [prependListener()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#prependlistener-1)

[Section titled “prependListener()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#prependlistener-1)

    prependListener<N>(eventName, listener): this

Adds the `listener` function to the _beginning_ of the listeners array for the event named `eventName`. No checks are made to see if the `listener` has already been added. Multiple calls passing the same combination of `eventName`and `listener` will result in the `listener` being added, and called, multiple times.

Returns a reference to the `EventEmitter`, so that calls can be chained.

| `N` _extends_ `string` \| `number` \| `symbol` |

Returns a reference to the `EventEmitter`, so that calls can be chained.

### [open()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#open)

[Section titled “open()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#open)

    function open(path, openWith?): Promise<void>

Opens a path or URL with the system’s default app, or the one specified with `openWith`.

The `openWith` value must be one of `firefox`, `google chrome`, `chromium` `safari`, `open`, `start`, `xdg-open`, `gio`, `gnome-open`, `kde-open` or `wslview`.

#### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#parameters-26)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#parameters-26)

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `path` | `string` | The path or URL to open. This value is matched against the string regex defined on `tauri.conf.json > plugins > shell > open`, which defaults to \`^((mailto:\\w+) |
| `openWith`? | `string` | The app to open the file or URL with. Defaults to the system default application for the specified path type. |

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#returns-30)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#returns-30)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`void`\>

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#example-11)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#example-11)

    import { open } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-shell';// opens the given URL on the default browser:await open('https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri');// opens the given URL using `firefox`:await open('https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri', 'firefox');// opens a file using the default program:await open('/path/to/file');

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#since-27)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/shell/#since-27)

2.0.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/shell/guest-js/index.ts#L601](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/shell/guest-js/index.ts#L601)

# @tauri-apps/plugin-notification | Tauri

[Skip to content](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#_top)

@tauri-apps/plugin-notification
===============================

Send toast notifications (brief auto-expiring OS window element) to your user. Can also be used with the Notification Web API.

[Enumerations](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#enumerations)

### [Options](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#options)

[Section titled “Options”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#options)

Options to send a notification.

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#since)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#since)

2.0.0

#### [Properties](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#properties-6)

[Section titled “Properties”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#properties-6)

| Property | Type | Description | Defined in |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `actionTypeId?` | `string` | Defines an action type for this notification. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L62](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L62) |
| `attachments?` | [`Attachment`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#attachment)<br>\[\] | Notification attachments. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L110](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L110) |
| `autoCancel?` | `boolean` | Automatically cancel the notification when the user clicks on it. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L126](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L126) |
| `body?` | `string` | Optional notification body. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L44](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L44) |
| `channelId?` | `string` | Identifier of the [Channel](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#channel)<br> that deliveres this notification. If the channel does not exist, the notification won’t fire. Make sure the channel exists with listChannels and [createChannel](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#createchannel)<br>. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L36](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L36) |
| `extra?` | [`Record`](https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/utility-types.html#recordkeys-type)<br><`string`, `unknown`\> | Extra payload to store in the notification. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L114](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L114) |
| `group?` | `string` | Identifier used to group multiple notifications. [https://developer.apple.com/documentation/usernotifications/unmutablenotificationcontent/1649872-threadidentifier](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/usernotifications/unmutablenotificationcontent/1649872-threadidentifier) | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L68](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L68) |
| `groupSummary?` | `boolean` | Instructs the system that this notification is the summary of a group on Android. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L72](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L72) |
| `icon?` | `string` | Notification icon. On Android the icon must be placed in the app’s `res/drawable` folder. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L96](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L96) |
| `iconColor?` | `string` | Icon color on Android. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L106](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L106) |
| `id?` | `number` | The notification identifier to reference this object later. Must be a 32-bit integer. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L29](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L29) |
| `inboxLines?` | `string`\[\] | List of lines to add to the notification. Changes the notification style to inbox. Cannot be used with `largeBody`. Only supports up to 5 lines. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L90](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L90) |
| `largeBody?` | `string` | Multiline text. Changes the notification style to big text. Cannot be used with `inboxLines`. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L54](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L54) |
| `largeIcon?` | `string` | Notification large icon (Android). The icon must be placed in the app’s `res/drawable` folder. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L102](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L102) |
| `number?` | `number` | Sets the number of items this notification represents on Android. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L138](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L138) |
| `ongoing?` | `boolean` | If true, the notification cannot be dismissed by the user on Android. An application service must manage the dismissal of the notification. It is typically used to indicate a background task that is pending (e.g. a file download) or the user is engaged with (e.g. playing music). | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L122](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L122) |
| `schedule?` | [`Schedule`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#schedule) | Schedule this notification to fire on a later time or a fixed interval. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L48](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L48) |
| `silent?` | `boolean` | Changes the notification presentation to be silent on iOS (no badge, no sound, not listed). | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L130](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L130) |
| `sound?` | `string` | The sound resource name or file path for the notification. Platform specific behavior: - On macOS: use system sounds (e.g., “Ping”, “Blow”) or sound files in the app bundle - On Linux: use XDG theme sounds (e.g., “message-new-instant”) or file paths - On Windows: use file paths to sound files (.wav format) - On Mobile: use resource names | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L82](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L82) |
| `summary?` | `string` | Detail text for the notification with `largeBody`, `inboxLines` or `groupSummary`. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L58](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L58) |
| `title` | `string` | Notification title. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L40](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L40) |
| `visibility?` | [`Visibility`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#visibility) | Notification visibility. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L134](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L134) |

* * *

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#since-10)

2.0.0

### [removeChannel()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#removechannel)

[Section titled “removeChannel()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#removechannel)

    function removeChannel(id): Promise<void>

Removes the channel with the given identifier.

#### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#parameters-9)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#parameters-9)

| Parameter | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `id` | `string` |

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#returns-16)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#returns-16)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`void`\>

A promise indicating the success or failure of the operation.

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#example-10)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#example-10)

    import { removeChannel } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-notification';await removeChannel();

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#since-11)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#since-11)

2.0.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L542](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L542)

* * *

### [requestPermission()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#requestpermission)

[Section titled “requestPermission()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#requestpermission)

    function requestPermission(): Promise<NotificationPermission>

Requests the permission to send notifications.

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#returns-17)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#returns-17)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`NotificationPermission`\>

A promise resolving to whether the user granted the permission or not.

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#example-11)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#example-11)

    import { isPermissionGranted, requestPermission } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-notification';let permissionGranted = await isPermissionGranted();if (!permissionGranted) {  const permission = await requestPermission();  permissionGranted = permission === 'granted';}

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#since-12)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#since-12)

2.0.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L348](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/notification/guest-js/index.ts#L348)

* * *

### [sendNotification()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#sendnotification)

[Section titled “sendNotification()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#sendnotification)

    function sendNotification(options): void

Sends a notification to the user.

#### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#parameters-10)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#parameters-10)

| Parameter | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `options` | `string` \| [`Options`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#options) |

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#returns-18)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#returns-18)

`void`

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#example-12)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#example-12)

    import { isPermissionGranted, requestPermission, sendNotification } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-notification';let permissionGranted = await isPermissionGranted();if (!permissionGranted) {  const permission = await requestPermission();  permissionGranted = permission === 'granted';}if (permissionGranted) {  sendNotification('Tauri is awesome!');  sendNotification({ title: 'TAURI', body: 'Tauri is awesome!' });}

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#since-13)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/notification/#since-13)

2.0.0

# path | Tauri

[Skip to content](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#_top)

path
====

The path module provides utilities for working with file and directory paths.

This package is also accessible with `window.__TAURI__.path` when [`app.withGlobalTauri`](https://v2.tauri.app/reference/config/#withglobaltauri)
 in `tauri.conf.json` is set to `true`.

It is recommended to allowlist only the APIs you use for optimal bundle size and security.

[Enumerations](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#enumerations)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### [appCacheDir()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#appcachedir)

[Section titled “appCacheDir()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#appcachedir)

    function appCacheDir(): Promise<string>

Returns the path to the suggested directory for your app’s cache files. Resolves to `${cacheDir}/${bundleIdentifier}`, where `bundleIdentifier` is the [`identifier`](https://v2.tauri.app/reference/config/#identifier)
 value configured in `tauri.conf.json`.

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#returns)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#returns)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`string`\>

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#example)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#example)

    import { appCacheDir } from '@tauri-apps/api/path';const appCacheDirPath = await appCacheDir();

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#since-1)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#since-1)

1.2.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri/blob/dev/packages/api/src/path.ts#L176](https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri/blob/dev/packages/api/src/path.ts#L176)

* * *

### [appConfigDir()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#appconfigdir)

[Section titled “appConfigDir()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#appconfigdir)

    function appConfigDir(): Promise<string>

Returns the path to the suggested directory for your app’s config files. Resolves to `${configDir}/${bundleIdentifier}`, where `bundleIdentifier` is the [`identifier`](https://v2.tauri.app/reference/config/#identifier)
 value configured in `tauri.conf.json`.

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#returns-1)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#returns-1)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`string`\>

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#example-1)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#example-1)

    import { appConfigDir } from '@tauri-apps/api/path';const appConfigDirPath = await appConfigDir();

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#since-2)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#since-2)

1.2.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri/blob/dev/packages/api/src/path.ts#L125](https://github.com/tauri-apps/tauri/blob/dev/packages/api/src/path.ts#L125)

### [appDataDir()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#appdatadir)

[Section titled “appDataDir()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#appdatadir)

    function appDataDir(): Promise<string>

Returns the path to the suggested directory for your app’s data files. Resolves to `${dataDir}/${bundleIdentifier}`, where `bundleIdentifier` is the [`identifier`](https://v2.tauri.app/reference/config/#identifier)
 value configured in `tauri.conf.json`.

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#returns-2)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#returns-2)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`string`\>

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#example-2)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#example-2)

    import { appDataDir } from '@tauri-apps/api/path';const appDataDirPath = await appDataDir();

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#since-3)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#since-3)

1.2.0

### [appLocalDataDir()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#applocaldatadir)

[Section titled “appLocalDataDir()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#applocaldatadir)

    function appLocalDataDir(): Promise<string>

Returns the path to the suggested directory for your app’s local data files. Resolves to `${localDataDir}/${bundleIdentifier}`, where `bundleIdentifier` is the [`identifier`](https://v2.tauri.app/reference/config/#identifier)
 value configured in `tauri.conf.json`.

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#returns-3)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#returns-3)

<`string`\>

### [appLogDir()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#applogdir)

[Section titled “appLogDir()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#applogdir)

    function appLogDir(): Promise<string>

Returns the path to the suggested directory for your app’s log files.

Platform-specific

*   **Linux:** Resolves to `${configDir}/${bundleIdentifier}/logs`.
*   **macOS:** Resolves to `${homeDir}/Library/Logs/{bundleIdentifier}`
*   **Windows:** Resolves to `${configDir}/${bundleIdentifier}/logs`.

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#returns-4)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#returns-4)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`string`\>

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#example-4)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#example-4)

    import { appLogDir } from '@tauri-apps/api/path';const appLogDirPath = await appLogDir();

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#since-5)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#since-5)

### [audioDir()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#audiodir)

[Section titled “audioDir()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#audiodir)

    function audioDir(): Promise<string>

Returns the path to the user’s audio directory.

Platform-specific

*   **Linux:** Resolves to [`xdg-user-dirs`](https://www.freedesktop.org/wiki/Software/xdg-user-dirs/)
    ’ `XDG_MUSIC_DIR`.
*   **macOS:** Resolves to `$HOME/Music`.
*   **Windows:** Resolves to `{FOLDERID_Music}`.

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#returns-5)

<`string`\>

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#example-5)

    import { audioDir } from '@tauri-apps/api/path';const audioDirPath = await audioDir();

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#since-6)

### [basename()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#basename)

[Section titled “basename()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#basename)

    function basename(path, ext?): Promise<string>

Returns the last portion of a `path`. Trailing directory separators are ignored.

#### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#parameters)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#parameters)

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `path` | `string` | \-  |
| `ext`? | `string` | An optional file extension to be removed from the returned path. |

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#returns-6)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#returns-6)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`string`\>

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#example-6)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#example-6)

    import { basename } from '@tauri-apps/api/path';const base = await basename('path/to/app.conf');assert(base === 'app.conf');

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#since-7)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#since-7)

1.0.0

### [cacheDir()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#cachedir)

[Section titled “cacheDir()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#cachedir)

    function cacheDir(): Promise<string>

Returns the path to the user’s cache directory.

Platform-specific

*   **Linux:** Resolves to `$XDG_CACHE_HOME` or `$HOME/.cache`.
*   **macOS:** Resolves to `$HOME/Library/Caches`.
*   **Windows:** Resolves to `{FOLDERID_LocalAppData}`.

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#returns-7)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#returns-7)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`string`\>

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#example-7)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#example-7)

    import { cacheDir } from '@tauri-apps/api/path';const cacheDirPath = await cacheDir();

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#since-8)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#since-8)

[Section titled “configDir()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#configdir)

    function configDir(): Promise<string>

Returns the path to the user’s config directory.

Platform-specific

*   **Linux:** Resolves to `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME` or `$HOME/.config`.
*   **macOS:** Resolves to `$HOME/Library/Application Support`.
*   **Windows:** Resolves to `{FOLDERID_RoamingAppData}`.

[Section titled “dataDir()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#datadir)

    function dataDir(): Promise<string>

Returns the path to the user’s data directory.

Platform-specific

*   **Linux:** Resolves to `$XDG_DATA_HOME` or `$HOME/.local/share`.
*   **macOS:** Resolves to `$HOME/Library/Application Support`.
*   **Windows:** Resolves to `{FOLDERID_RoamingAppData}`.

Platform-specific

    ’ `XDG_DESKTOP_DIR`.
*   **macOS:** Resolves to `$HOME/Desktop`.
*   **Windows:** Resolves to `{FOLDERID_Desktop}`.

    function dirname(path): Promise<string>

Returns the parent directory of a given `path`. Trailing directory separators are ignored.

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#parameters-1)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#example-11)

    import { dirname } from '@tauri-apps/api/path';const dir = await dirname('/path/to/somedir/');assert(dir === '/path/to');

### [executableDir()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#executabledir)

[Section titled “executableDir()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#executabledir)

    function executableDir(): Promise<string>

Returns the path to the user’s executable directory.

Platform-specific

*   **Linux:** Resolves to `$XDG_BIN_HOME/../bin` or `$XDG_DATA_HOME/../bin` or `$HOME/.local/bin`.
*   **macOS:** Not supported.
*   **Windows:** Not supported.

### [join()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#join)

[Section titled “join()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#join)

    function join(...paths): Promise<string>

Joins all given `path` segments together using the platform-specific separator as a delimiter, then normalizes the resulting path.

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#parameters-4)

<`string`\>

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#example-19)

    import { join, appDataDir } from '@tauri-apps/api/path';const appDataDirPath = await appDataDir();const path = await join(appDataDirPath, 'users', 'tauri', 'avatar.png');

### [localDataDir()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#localdatadir)

[Section titled “localDataDir()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#localdatadir)

    function localDataDir(): Promise<string>

Returns the path to the user’s local data directory.

Platform-specific

*   **Linux:** Resolves to `$XDG_DATA_HOME` or `$HOME/.local/share`.
*   **macOS:** Resolves to `$HOME/Library/Application Support`.
*   **Windows:** Resolves to `{FOLDERID_LocalAppData}`.

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#returns-21)

<`string`\>

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#example-20)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#since-22)

### [resourceDir()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#resourcedir)

[Section titled “resourceDir()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#resourcedir)

    function resourceDir(): Promise<string>

Returns the path to the application’s resource directory. To resolve a resource path, see [`resolveResource`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#resolveresource)
.

[Platform-specific](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#platform-specific)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Section titled “Platform-specific”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#platform-specific)

Although we provide the exact path where this function resolves to, this is not a contract and things might change in the future

*   **Windows:** Resolves to the directory that contains the main executable.
*   **Linux:** When running in an AppImage, the `APPDIR` variable will be set to the mounted location of the app, and the resource dir will be `${APPDIR}/usr/lib/${exe_name}`. If not running in an AppImage, the path is `/usr/lib/${exe_name}`. When running the app from `src-tauri/target/(debug|release)/`, the path is `${exe_dir}/../lib/${exe_name}`.
*   **macOS:** Resolves to `${exe_dir}/../Resources` (inside .app).
*   **iOS:** Resolves to `${exe_dir}/assets`.
*   **Android:** Currently the resources are stored in the APK as assets so it’s not a normal file system path, we return a special URI prefix `asset://localhost/` here that can be used with the [file system plugin](https://tauri.app/plugin/file-system/)
    ,

#### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#returns-27)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#returns-27)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`string`\>

#### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#example-26)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#example-26)

    import { resourceDir } from '@tauri-apps/api/path';const resourceDirPath = await resourceDir();

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#since-28)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#since-28)

# @tauri-apps/plugin-fs | Tauri

[Skip to content](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#_top)

@tauri-apps/plugin-fs
=====================

Access the file system.

[Security](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#security)

----------------------------------------------------------------

[Section titled “Security”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#security)

This module prevents path traversal, not allowing parent directory accessors to be used (i.e. “/usr/path/to/../file” or ”../path/to/file” paths are not allowed). Paths accessed with this API must be either relative to one of the [base directories](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#basedirectory)
 or created with the [path API](https://v2.tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/)
.

The API has a scope configuration that forces you to restrict the paths that can be accessed using glob patterns.

The scope configuration is an array of glob patterns describing file/directory paths that are allowed. For instance, this scope configuration allows **all** enabled `fs` APIs to (only) access files in the _databases_ directory of the [`$APPDATA` directory](https://v2.tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#appdatadir)
:

    {  "permissions": [    {      "identifier": "fs:scope",      "allow": [{ "path": "$APPDATA/databases/*" }]    }  ]}

Scopes can also be applied to specific `fs` APIs by using the API’s identifier instead of `fs:scope`:

    {  "permissions": [    {      "identifier": "fs:allow-exists",      "allow": [{ "path": "$APPDATA/databases/*" }]    }  ]}

Notice the use of the `$APPDATA` variable. The value is injected at runtime, resolving to the [app data directory](https://v2.tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#appdatadir)
.

The available variables are: [`$APPCONFIG`](https://v2.tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#appconfigdir)
, [`$APPDATA`](https://v2.tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#appdatadir)
, [`$APPLOCALDATA`](https://v2.tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#applocaldatadir)
, [`$APPCACHE`](https://v2.tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#appcachedir)
, [`$APPLOG`](https://v2.tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#applogdir)
, [`$AUDIO`](https://v2.tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#audiodir)
, [`$CACHE`](https://v2.tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#cachedir)
, [`$CONFIG`](https://v2.tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#configdir)
, [`$DATA`](https://v2.tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#datadir)
, [`$LOCALDATA`](https://v2.tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#localdatadir)
, [`$DESKTOP`](https://v2.tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#desktopdir)
, [`$DOCUMENT`](https://v2.tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#documentdir)
, [`$DOWNLOAD`](https://v2.tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#downloaddir)
, [`$EXE`](https://v2.tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#executabledir)
, [`$FONT`](https://v2.tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#fontdir)
, [`$HOME`](https://v2.tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#homedir)
, [`$PICTURE`](https://v2.tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#picturedir)
, [`$PUBLIC`](https://v2.tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#publicdir)
, [`$RUNTIME`](https://v2.tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#runtimedir)
, [`$TEMPLATE`](https://v2.tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#templatedir)
, [`$VIDEO`](https://v2.tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#videodir)
, [`$RESOURCE`](https://v2.tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#resourcedir)
, [`$TEMP`](https://v2.tauri.app/reference/javascript/api/namespacepath/#tempdir)
.

Trying to execute any API with a URL not configured on the scope results in a promise rejection due to denied access.

##### [close()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#close)

[Section titled “close()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#close)

    close(): Promise<void>

Destroys and cleans up this resource from memory. **You should not call any method on this object anymore and should drop any reference to it.**

###### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#returns-2)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#returns-2)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`void`\>

###### [Inherited from](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#inherited-from-2)

[Section titled “Inherited from”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#inherited-from-2)

`Resource.close`

**Source**: undefined

##### [read()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#read)

[Section titled “read()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#read)

    read(buffer): Promise<null | number>

Reads up to `p.byteLength` bytes into `p`. It resolves to the number of bytes read (`0` < `n` <= `p.byteLength`) and rejects if any error encountered. Even if `read()` resolves to `n` < `p.byteLength`, it may use all of `p` as scratch space during the call. If some data is available but not `p.byteLength` bytes, `read()` conventionally resolves to what is available instead of waiting for more.

When `read()` encounters end-of-file condition, it resolves to EOF (`null`).

When `read()` encounters an error, it rejects with an error.

Callers should always process the `n` > `0` bytes returned before considering the EOF (`null`). Doing so correctly handles I/O errors that happen after reading some bytes and also both of the allowed EOF behaviors.

###### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#parameters-1)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#parameters-1)

| Parameter | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `buffer` | [`Uint8Array`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Uint8Array) |

###### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#returns-3)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#returns-3)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`null` | `number`\>

###### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#example)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#example)

    import { open, BaseDirectory } from "@tauri-apps/plugin-fs"// if "$APPCONFIG/foo/bar.txt" contains the text "hello world":const file = await open("foo/bar.txt", { baseDir: BaseDirectory.AppConfig });const buf = new Uint8Array(100);const numberOfBytesRead = await file.read(buf); // 11 bytesconst text = new TextDecoder().decode(buf);  // "hello world"await file.close();

###### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#since-2)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#since-2)

2.0.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/fs/guest-js/index.ts#L314](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/fs/guest-js/index.ts#L314)

##### [seek()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#seek)

[Section titled “seek()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#seek)

    seek(offset, whence): Promise<number>

Seek sets the offset for the next `read()` or `write()` to offset, interpreted according to `whence`: `Start` means relative to the start of the file, `Current` means relative to the current offset, and `End` means relative to the end. Seek resolves to the new offset relative to the start of the file.

Seeking to an offset before the start of the file is an error. Seeking to any positive offset is legal, but the behavior of subsequent I/O operations on the underlying object is implementation-dependent. It returns the number of cursor position.

###### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#parameters-2)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#parameters-2)

| Parameter | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `offset` | `number` |
| `whence` | [`SeekMode`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#seekmode) |

###### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#returns-4)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#returns-4)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`number`\>

###### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#example-1)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#example-1)

    import { open, SeekMode, BaseDirectory } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-fs';
    // Given hello.txt pointing to file with "Hello world", which is 11 bytes long:const file = await open('hello.txt', { read: true, write: true, truncate: true, create: true, baseDir: BaseDirectory.AppLocalData });await file.write(new TextEncoder().encode("Hello world"));
    // Seek 6 bytes from the start of the fileconsole.log(await file.seek(6, SeekMode.Start)); // "6"// Seek 2 more bytes from the current positionconsole.log(await file.seek(2, SeekMode.Current)); // "8"// Seek backwards 2 bytes from the end of the fileconsole.log(await file.seek(-2, SeekMode.End)); // "9" (e.g. 11-2)
    await file.close();

###### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#since-3)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#since-3)

2.0.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/fs/guest-js/index.ts#L369](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/fs/guest-js/index.ts#L369)

##### [stat()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#stat)

[Section titled “stat()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#stat)

    stat(): Promise<FileInfo>

Returns a [`FileInfo`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#fileinfo)
 for this file.

###### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#returns-5)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#returns-5)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<[`FileInfo`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#fileinfo)
\>

###### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#example-2)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#example-2)

    import { open, BaseDirectory } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-fs';const file = await open("file.txt", { read: true, baseDir: BaseDirectory.AppLocalData });const fileInfo = await file.stat();console.log(fileInfo.isFile); // trueawait file.close();

###### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#since-4)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#since-4)

2.0.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/fs/guest-js/index.ts#L391](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/fs/guest-js/index.ts#L391)

##### [truncate()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#truncate)

[Section titled “truncate()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#truncate)

    truncate(len?): Promise<void>

Truncates or extends this file, to reach the specified `len`. If `len` is not specified then the entire file contents are truncated.

###### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#parameters-3)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#parameters-3)

| Parameter | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `len`? | `number` |

###### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#returns-6)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#returns-6)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`void`\>

###### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#example-3)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#example-3)

    import { open, BaseDirectory } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-fs';
    // truncate the entire fileconst file = await open("my_file.txt", { read: true, write: true, create: true, baseDir: BaseDirectory.AppLocalData });await file.truncate();
    // truncate part of the fileconst file = await open("my_file.txt", { read: true, write: true, create: true, baseDir: BaseDirectory.AppLocalData });await file.write(new TextEncoder().encode("Hello World"));await file.truncate(7);const data = new Uint8Array(32);await file.read(data);console.log(new TextDecoder().decode(data)); // Hello Wawait file.close();

###### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#since-5)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#since-5)

2.0.0

**Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/fs/guest-js/index.ts#L423](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/fs/guest-js/index.ts#L423)

##### [write()](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#write)

[Section titled “write()”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#write)

    write(data): Promise<number>

Writes `data.byteLength` bytes from `data` to the underlying data stream. It resolves to the number of bytes written from `data` (`0` <= `n` <= `data.byteLength`) or reject with the error encountered that caused the write to stop early. `write()` must reject with a non-null error if would resolve to `n` < `data.byteLength`. `write()` must not modify the slice data, even temporarily.

###### [Parameters](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#parameters-4)

[Section titled “Parameters”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#parameters-4)

| Parameter | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `data` | [`Uint8Array`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Uint8Array) |

###### [Returns](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#returns-7)

[Section titled “Returns”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#returns-7)

[`Promise`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise)
<`number`\>

###### [Example](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#example-4)

[Section titled “Example”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#example-4)

    import { open, write, BaseDirectory } from '@tauri-apps/plugin-fs';const encoder = new TextEncoder();const data = encoder.encode("Hello world");const file = await open("bar.txt", { write: true, baseDir: BaseDirectory.AppLocalData });const bytesWritten = await file.write(data); // 11await file.close();

###### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#since-6)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#since-6)

2.0.0

### [DirEntry](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#direntry)

[Section titled “DirEntry”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#direntry)

A disk entry which is either a file, a directory or a symlink.

This is the result of the [`readDir`](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#readdir)
.

#### [Since](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#since-10)

[Section titled “Since”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#since-10)

2.0.0

#### [Properties](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#properties-3)

[Section titled “Properties”](https://tauri.app/reference/javascript/fs/#properties-3)

| Property | Type | Description | Defined in |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `isDirectory` | `boolean` | Specifies whether this entry is a directory or not. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/fs/guest-js/index.ts#L677](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/fs/guest-js/index.ts#L677) |
| `isFile` | `boolean` | Specifies whether this entry is a file or not. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/fs/guest-js/index.ts#L679](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/fs/guest-js/index.ts#L679) |
| `isSymlink` | `boolean` | Specifies whether this entry is a symlink or not. | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/fs/guest-js/index.ts#L681](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/fs/guest-js/index.ts#L681) |
| `name` | `string` | The name of the entry (file name with extension or directory name). | **Source**: [https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/fs/guest-js/index.ts#L675](https://github.com/tauri-apps/plugins-workspace/blob/v2/plugins/fs/guest-js/index.ts#L675) |

* * *
